PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
3. DESCRIPTION OF THE VENTURE
3.1 INTRODUCTION
The proposed venture in its global development foresees the construction and operation of a
data center building, which will be installed in four stages, and other auxiliary facilities. The
environmental analysis will be carried out for the undertaking in its complete development, that
is, considering the vision of global construction and the operation of the data center building at
its maximum capacity, since it is the most conservative scenario from the environmental point
of view.
The project will have a construction phase, an operation and maintenance phase, and a potential
closure phase. Likewise, when installed in stages, there is the particularity that the operation
phase of one stage can overlap with the construction phase of the next. The exact timeline of
development of the phases for each stage will depend on the future needs of the venture,
according to the demand for the service.
In turn, the operation phase is divided into normal operation and emergency operation, as
explained below.
Given the operational characteristics of the proposed venture, it is expected that its operation
will be uninterrupted, that is, it will operate 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. By virtue of the
foregoing, it will require a continuous supply of energy, which will be provided by UTE's
electricity supply system, based on an electrical substation located inside the property of the
proposed enterprise, as the main supply and during its normal operation; and emergency
generators as backup during emergency operation. The emergency generators will operate on
diesel fuel.
The supply of energy is considered a critical aspect of the operations of the enterprise, which
are carried out with the expectation that there will be no interruptions in this supply by the UTE
network. The project incorporates substantial measures to increase the reliability of the
electricity supply infrastructure. The occurrence of unforeseen on-site or off-site events that
would trigger the operation of a single or several emergency generators will be unlikely and
typically short-lived.
Additionally, the project owner has extensive experience in planning the continuity of its
operations, and will have trained personnel at all times who will respond quickly, minimizing the
need for the use of generators. Even in the face of a hypothetical extraordinary extended
operation under power outage, the project can reduce backup power generation by
implementing protocols that gradually alleviate power demand. Based on an analysis of the UTE
system, its reliability and the improvements planned under this proposed undertaking,
emergency power outages of the UTE system requiring the operation of generators are expected
to occur less than once per year.
The components of the proposed undertaking and land cover are described below, and each of
the above phases is described in the following chapters.
13

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
3.2 COMPONENT DESCRIPTION
Overview
The main component of the proposed venture is the data center building, and there will be
specific infrastructure on the site to service it. Also considered within the scope of the proposed
undertaking is the infrastructure to be built to connect to PDLC's domestic wastewater
treatment plant.
The following list presents the components of the proposed venture.
 Data Center Building (DC-01).
 Cooling system for the data center building.
 Electrical substation.
 Electric yard for data center building (EYD).
 Mechanical yard for data center building (MYD).
 Interconnection Building (SLTE).
 Administration buildings and services (HUB and facilities service area).
 Auxiliary infrastructure.
In the Figure 3-1 a general outline of the proposed undertaking is presented and a general
detailed plan is presented in the Annex I.
Figure 3-1: General scheme of entrepreneurship.
The maximum heights that the buildings and all infrastructure to be installed in all phases of the
proposed undertaking will comply with the current regulations of the Municipality of Canelones.
Additionally, while the proposed venture is close to Carrasco International Airport, the use of
airspace is of relevance, especially in the design stages. This leads to the proposed undertaking
being formulated in addition considering the corresponding regulatory framework for which the
14

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
pertinent procedures will be carried out before the DINACIA. The maximum height of the
proposed development will be 57.9 m above mean sea level.
Data Center Building
The venture is expected to have a data center building, called DC-01.
Inside this building there will be the necessary equipment for the distribution of electrical energy
and the cooling system to maintain the environment at the required operating temperatures.
There will also be service facilities for staff, such as offices, bathrooms, kitchenette and sectors
for the temporary collection of waste.
Cooling system
The cooling system of the data center building will consist of 32 air-cooled chillers, with closed
water circuits, arranged in 7+1 mode for each module of the data center. The closed-loop system
consists of cooling water recirculation and consists of distribution pipes within the data center,
pumps and heat exchangers. The cooling water circulating through the pipes absorbs the
ambient heat generated by the servers, thus lowering the ambient temperature of the data
center.
Other cooling systems (and air conditioning) will also operate on the premises for secondary
functions. For example, HVAC systems with variable refrigerant flow (VRF) and air handling units
(AHU).
Electrical substation
The proposed project will have a 150 kV UTE maneuvering substation, and adjacent to it, there
will also be its own substation, both located on the east side of the property. The UTE substation
will be designed to receive two circuits in 150 kV, which have Prior Environmental Authorization
according to Ministerial Resolution 255/2021 of April 16, 2021.
The UTE substation will be arranged inside a building and with a modular GIS type equipment
("Gas Insulated Switchgear") isolated in SF6. Leaving it two circuits of 150 kV in underground
cable to the own substation.
Within the substation itself, the voltage will be reduced to the distribution voltage level (22 kV),
through two transformers that feed the data centers.
Along with the substation of the property, electrical yards, medium voltage rooms and a control
room will be installed.
Electric yard
The proposed venture includes an electric yard (EYD), installed in stages as each area of the data
center is installed. This sector will include electrical connections both for the supply of energy
from the substation and from the emergency generators.
Critical generators, which will provide backup supply to the data center building in the event of
an on-site power outage, will be positioned to align with data center equipment. The generators
will be housed in enclosures and are designed with individual double-walled diesel storage tanks
that are recharged at dedicated fueling stations on site. There will be generators located in the
15

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
power yard, each one will have a chimney for the exhaust of the combustion gases. The stacks
comply with local Environmental emission regulations.
ANNEX III presents a report that provides background and technical information to justify the
selection of the best emission control technology available for emergency generators, through
the use of a top-down process.
Mechanical yard (MYD)
The project includes a mechanical yard, installed in stages as each area of the data center is
installed. This sector shall consist of the cooling system, described in section 3.2.3, and the
emergency generators of the cooling system.
There will be emergency generators in the mechanical yard, which will allow the operation of all
the components of the cooling system even in the event of power cuts. Each will have a chimney
for the exhaust of combustion gases. The stacks comply with local Environmental emission
regulations.
Additionally, there will be cooling system service facilities, such as the chemical water
conditioning plant for closed circuits, a water storage tank, chemical input tank, pump houses,
chemical input and fuel discharge areas, and various cooling equipment for the office building.
Similarly to electrical yards, mechanical yards will have the connection to the electricity supply
both from the electrical substation and from the emergency generators.
Interconnection building
This small building will contain the line-terminating telecommunications equipment required to
act as an interface between the data centers and the fiber optic cable entering the site. This
building will also have electricity distribution and cooling services for telecommunications
equipment.
Administration Building
The proposed venture will have a building entirely dedicated to housing administrative tasks
that will include a dining room, bathrooms, offices and meeting rooms. It will have a small
backup electric generator in case of emergency, and parking spaces attached to the building.
Auxiliary infrastructure
Water supply
All the water used by the project is supplied by PDLC's drinking water network.
General Drinking Water
The drinking water for general use will be stored in a tank located near the limit of the property
with Otorgués Street. Through this tank the water will be pumped and distributed to the
administration buildings, interconnection, personnel areas and services within the data center
building.
16

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
The drinking water circuit pipes surrounding the blocks will be provided by PDLC under the scope
of your SPG.
Water from the fire detection and extinguishing system
The water distribution network for the firefighting system will be fed from a water tank by means
of a pump station, located in the West sector of the property, for exclusive use for this purpose.
The distribution network will cover the data center, electrical and mechanical yards,
administrative buildings and interconnection building. The network will have hydrants and
sprinklers.
The firefighting water circuit pipes surrounding the blocks will be provided by PDLC under the
scope of its SPG.
Wastewater network
Domestic waste water
Domestic wastewater will be conducted (by gravity whenever possible) from the various
generation points to a pumping station located on site. From the pumping station they will be
pumped to the Wastewater Treatment Plant (hereinafter WWTP) of PDLC, by a impulsion line.
Although the last part of the drive line is off-site, it falls within this environmental authorization.
ANNEX II presents the floor plan of the impulsion of wastewater assimilable to domestic from
the limit of the property of the proposed enterprise to the entrance chamber of the PDLC
WWTP.
The pipes of the domestic wastewater circuit surrounding the blocks will be provided by PDLC
under the scope of its SPG.
Industrial wastewater
Industrial wastewater will be generated in scheduled maintenance activities in the closed cycle
cooling system, or by valve breaks, seals, etc., in this system. This wastewater will be collected
in tanks and then sent to authorized managers.
Drains and buffer lagoons
There will be a rainwater collection and conduction system through pipes and ditches up to two
buffer lagoons provided for on the property, one next to the northern limit of the property and
the other next to the southern limit. The rainwater collection system associated with the DC-01
and its associated facilities will be conducted to the South buffer lagoon. The lagoon that will be
located in the North will discharge into the Gasser Glen, while the one that will be located in the
South limit will discharge into an existing culvert on the site, ending in the municipal drainage
system.
It should be noted that the construction of the buffer lagoons, as well as the main drainage roads
and pipelines around the main blocks of the project are under the scope of the expansion of
PDLC.
Mains
The electrical grid within the site will be composed primarily of power cables installed on trestles
from the substation to the electrical yard and mechanical yard of the data center. The
connections to the administration buildings, telecommunications and drinking water pumping
stations (general use and fire protection) will be by underground cable through connections at
different points from the substation.
17

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
The electrical system will have medium voltage rooms that will function as an interface,
receiving electrical energy from the electrical substation and distributing it to the distribution
sites that will feed the servers within the data centers.
Telecommunications network
The telecommunications network distributes the fiber optic laying from the interconnection
building to the data center, the electrical substation and the administration building, through an
underground pipeline.
The circuit of the telecommunications line surrounding the blocks will be provided by PDLC
under the scope of its SPG.
Fire detection and extinguishing system
The fire detection system will operate by means of sensors installed within the sectors as
required by the authorization of the National Fire Department (hereinafter DNB) to be managed.
This detection system will operate independently of the general power grid, connecting at the
same time with the emergency electricity generation system, thus ensuring operation whenever
there is an emergency.
Access gate
The access control gate to the property of the proposed development will be located on the
northwest limit, at the proposed main access.
Internal paths
The internal roads will connect the different facilities of the proposed venture with each other.
Parking lots
It is planned to have a parking lot in front of the data center building, on the east side of the
property, and a parking lot in front of the administration building, on the west side of the
property. The minimum capacity will be 40 vehicles. Covered parking for bicycles is also planned.
Loading and unloading areas
It is expected to have areas for loading and unloading inputs in different locations of the
property.
All fuel loading areas for emergency generators will have grid grates located below the fuel
loading points. These gullies will drain into a hydrocarbon separator (with high oil level alarms),
which will have sufficient capacity to contain the entire volume of a section of the fuel tanker.
Additionally, there will be a chemical input discharge area in the mechanical yard associated
with the chemical treatment of water from the closed circuit of the cooling system, with
containment provided for potential spills.
3.3 LAND COVER
The proposed venture will consist of impervious land surfaces, consisting of data center
buildings, electrical substation, administration building, and impervious areas not built, such as
internal roads, parking lots, input loading and unloading areas, mechanical yard and electrical
yard, etc. These impervious areas will be located mainly on the South, Southwest of the
property, on the register No. 47,763 and the registers that are added (Nos. 47,827 to No.
47,832), as mentioned in Section 1.5.

4. CONSTRUCTION PHASE
4.1 DESCRIPTION OF THE CONSTRUCTION STAGES ON THE SITE
Introduction
The construction stages associated with this environmental authorization are described below.
As a general concept, it should be noted that the main works of conditioning of the property,
corresponding to leveling and stabilization of the soil, blockage and basic internal roads, are
included within the environmental authorization requested by PDLC for the expansion of its Free
Trade Zone. Within this conditioning is also the primary laying of the basic auxiliary
infrastructure offered by a Free Trade Zone, in particular the drinking water distribution network
and firefighting network, rainwater management, collection and impulsion of water assimilable
to domestic, and telecommunications network, to which the proposed venture will be
connected.
Within the scope of the proposed undertaking, the data center building and its associated
services will be interconnected with the infrastructure of the PDLC free zone mentioned above,
and all the tasks of final conditioning of the property will also be carried out, with the paving of
streets, pedestrian circulation paths and green areas, among others.
It should be noted that the environmental impact assessment is done for the entire project.
Stages
The construction of the project will be carried out in stages, determined mainly by the civil works
of the data center building. This is divided into the construction of modular zones that,
combined, end up forming a single operating unit. Thus, a modular concept is considered for
assembly activities.
The work schedule was designed in such a way that, once the works of a stage are completed, it
is able to enter into operation. Therefore, the operation phase of one stage may overlap with
the construction of the next.
The progress of construction tasks such as assembly will be determined by the needs of the
business.
Stage 1
At this stage, the circulation routes (including pedestrian routes) and service routes are installed,
foreseeing the installation of future stages (the underground service infrastructure is designed
for the total capacity of the project). Stage 1 comprises:
 Construction of underground service facilities for all stages (electricity, drinking water,
sewage, telecommunications, etc.).
 Construction of piles and foundation braces for all the necessary components of the 4
stages.
 Construction of concrete slabs for the building of the facilities support area, and for
modules of stages 1 and 2.
 Entrance and circulation roads, security booth, drinking water tanks, firefighting water,
pumping facilities, etc.
20

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Construction of the internal electrical substation.
 Construction of the first module of the data center building (consisting of a fraction of
the total power to be installed) together with the mechanical and electrical
components, support facilities and infrastructure necessary for its operation
(administration and services, electric yard, mechanical yard, cooling system, etc.).
 Construction of the structure of Stage 2, unequipped.
 Construction and equipment of the facilities service area.
 Installation of auxiliary infrastructure of the proposed enterprise:
o Line of impulsion of wastewater assimilable to domestic from the limit of the
property of the proposed project to the WWTP of PDLC.
Stage 2
This stage consists of the electromechanical assembly of the second module of the data center
building (another fraction of the total power to be installed is added) together with the
associated mechanical and electrical components, the support facilities and infrastructure
necessary for its operation (electrical and mechanical yard, cooling system, etc.). The
connections to the services for this stage were foreseen in the previous stage.
Stage 3
This stage consists of the construction of the slabs and structure for modules 3 and 4, and the
electromechanical assembly of the third module of the data center building (another fraction of
the total power to be installed is added) along with its associated services (electrical and
mechanical yard, cooling system, etc.).
Stage 4
This last stage consists of the electromechanical assembly of the fourth and last module of the
data center building (completing the power to be installed) together with its associated services
(electrical and mechanical yard, cooling system, etc.), as well as the data interconnection
building (SLTE), the administration building (HUB) and parking lots, and landscaping (streets,
gardening, etc.).
4.2 WORK SCHEDULE
Schedule of works on site
The works and commissioning corresponding to Stage 1 on site would last approximately 26
months. It is worth clarifying that there could be overlap between the end of the soil movement
stage associated with the expansion of PDLC, with the start of the foundation and paving works
associated with the proposed project.
For the installation works of the line of impulsion of the effluents assimilable to domestic to the
WWTP of PDLC, a duration of approximately 9 months is foreseen, and 2 more months for the
commissioning.
In the Board 4-1 The overall schedule of works is presented, not including off-site or soil
movement works. Likewise, since the schedule of works will be driven by business requirements,
the indicated deadlines are approximate and are subject to change. If demand is higher than
expected, it may be necessary to execute the works more quickly. Conversely, if demand is lower
than expected, there could be longer periods between stages.

4.3 DESCRIPTION OF WORKS ON SITE
Introduction
This chapter describes the main construction activities.
Land leveling
As mentioned above, the main works of conditioning of the property, corresponding to leveling
and stabilization of the soil, blockage and basic internal roads, are included within the
environmental authorization requested by PDLC for the expansion of its Free Trade Zone. Minor
soil movements will be carried out within the framework of the undertaking under study for the
fine adjustment of ground levels.
Civil works on the property
On the leveling works carried out by PDLC, the civil works of the proposed undertaking will begin,
considering those detailed below:
 Sidewalks, ramps, concrete stalls, roads, landscape, parking lots.
 Foundations for buildings and equipment (piles and beams).
 External and internal reinforced concrete slabs for buildings, mechanical yard and
electric yard.
 Auxiliary service infrastructure complementary to that previously existing (extension of
the PDLC): underground service facilities for all stages (electricity, drinking water,
sewage, telecommunications, etc.).
 Assembly and enclosure of steel structures of buildings and electrical and mechanical
yards.
The works will be carried out in the stages described in Section 4.1.
Electrical substation
The works associated with the construction of the substation are detailed below:
 Perimeter closure of the substation.
 Installation of mesh for grounding of the substation.
 Construction and assembly of equipment support.
 Construction of internal roads and drains.
 Construction and installation of lighting and security systems.
 Implementation of protection and telecommunications backup systems.
 Construction and assembly of low structures.
 Assembly and construction of substation equipment to receive the UTE system circuit.
 Construction of transformation infrastructure.
 Construction of substation control rooms.
Electromechanical works on the property
This set of activities includes the assembly of the equipment and metal structures of the works
or facilities and equipment that make up the project. The mechanical works that will be
developed are the following:
23

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Installation of electrical equipment to support the cooling system and the load of the
data centers.
 Assembly of emergency generators, with their respective integrated fuel tank and pipes
and valves necessary to distribute and supply fuel to emergency generators. The pipes
will be built above ground level and on trestle supports.
 Installation of air conditioning and heating equipment.
 Assembly of pipes and valves necessary for the cooling system, in addition to air
conditioning and heating.
 Assembly of the air cooling system ("chillers").
The assembly or installation of the different works and equipment will be carried out by means
of cranes that will comply with the maximum height restriction criteria imposed by the DINACIA
for this activity.
Instrumentation works on the premises
The instrumentation works include the installation of sensors in the data center, and
complementary works or equipment. The main sensors are level, leak detection sensors,
temperature, flow sensors, pressure sensors, current sensors, optical. At the same time, the
installation of the automatic control system for the cooling system and for the regular and
emergency electricity supply systems is considered.
Site Commissioning
Once the construction works have been executed, prior to the operation phase of each stage,
the commissioning tests will begin. In this instance, the systems will be conditioned and tests
will be carried out to ensure the correct functioning of the works and equipment, the electricity
supply, the cooling system, the emergency generators and all the associated critical
infrastructure.
Auxiliary infrastructure
Introduction
Within the works associated with the construction of the auxiliary infrastructure, typical works
of water, sanitation and drainage distribution networks, and works related to the electricity lines
are identified.
Water, sanitation and drainage distribution networks
It includes the construction of drinking water, sanitation and drainage networks, including soil
movements, laying pipes in trenches, filling, pipe protection slabs if necessary, construction of
chambers, placement of valves and accessories, etc.
In particular, the works corresponding to the last part of the pipeline for the discharge of
wastewater assimilable to domestic to the PDLC WWTP involve the installation of pressure
pipes, covering activities of soil movements, laying pipes in trenches, filling, installation of pipe
protection slabs if necessary, construction of chambers, placement of valves and fittings, etc.
The activities associated with the installation of pressure pipes are described below:
 Stakeout of the works.
 Pavement removal, trenching and pipe positioning.
 Placement and compaction of a support layer of granular or sandy material.
24

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Installation of pipes.
 Filling with classified material from the trench or brought from deposit.
Mains
The electrical works will be those detailed below:
 Supply and assembly of electrical equipment, such as electrical circuits, panels,
transformers, among others.
 Overhead trays and pipes between the substation, electromechanical equipment and
data rooms.
 Supply and assembly of lighting luminaires.
 Supply and assembly of security and access control systems.
 Construction and connection of grounding meshes of metal structures.
4.4 WORKSHOPS
Description
Offices and staff services
It is planned to install modules consisting of containers or light structures for use as offices,
dining room, changing rooms and toilets.
Storage of supplies and workshops
There will be areas for the storage of the necessary supplies for construction, as well as areas
for welding, carpentry, painting, among others.
Internal paths
The internal roads will not be paved during construction, so special attention will be taken to
keep them moist. Likewise, signage will be installed to control the speed inside the site and in
order to keep controlled the emissions of particulate matter, generated by these activities.
Parking lots
There will be an area for parking lots intended mainly for vehicles to be used during construction
and for personnel vehicles.
Waste storage sites
There will be separate storage sites for domestic, hazardous and non-hazardous waste
generated during construction.
Deposit of chemical inputs
A warehouse will be built for the storage of chemical inputs. This installation will be
implemented according to the characteristics of the inputs it will contain. It will also have the
Safety Sheets of the supplies, extinguishers in case of emergency and spill control system, for
those supplies with potential for spillage.
Location of workshop components for works on the premises
In principle, input depots, workshops, administration offices, parking lots, among others, during
the construction phase would be located on site in the areas adjacent to the initial construction
area. In case it is necessary to install some components of the workshop off-site, it will be agreed
with PDLC.
25

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
These areas will be bordered by temporary rain gutters and temporary internal roads.
4.5 PERSONNEL
For construction it is estimated that between 300 and 400 people will be employed on average,
with a peak of approximately 800 people.
Of the total personnel employed, a maximum of 25% of personnel of foreign origin is expected,
with the remaining 75% mostly coming from Montevideo and nearby towns. There are no plans
to install temporary accommodation. These proportions may vary during the progress of the
project.
The distribution of personnel hired by job profile varies during the progress of the project, and
includes engineers (civil/electrical/mechanical), construction officers (electricians, mechanics,
etc.), construction workers, specialized and non-specialized operators, site assistants,
administrative, cleaning personnel, guards, preventionists, supervisors, managers, etc.
4.6 MACHINERY
The machinery to be used during the construction phase on the site of the proposed project,
once the leveling and stabilization of the land on the site is completed, is as follows:
 Excavator.
 Backhoe.
 Truck.
 Tanker.
 Grader.
 Roller compactor.
 Bulldozer.
 Recycler and soil stabilizer.
 Tower crane.
 Mobile crane.
The tower crane and mobile crane will meet DINACIA's height requirements.
During the hours of activity of the works the mobile crane will be in different points of the
property depending on the degree of progress of the works and the type of task to be
performed.
In turn, the machinery to be used for the works corresponding to the network of impulsion of
wastewater assimilable to domestic towards the PDLC WWTP is detailed below:
 Backhoe without tires.
 Combined backhoe without tires.
 Dump truck.
 Tanker Truck or Shooting Tanker.
 Compressor.
 Vibrorammers.

4.8 INPUTS
Electrical energy
Power supply for construction activities will be provided through a PDLC grid timeline. There will
be generators distributed on-site for failure events.
Water
There will be water storage tanks supplied by tanker trucks for construction activities. On the
other hand, drinking water for staff use will be supplied through a temporary connection to the
OSE network. The use of drinking water dispensers is also considered a possibility.
Fuel
In the construction phase the main fuel will be diesel for the operation of machinery. No fuel
storage is foreseen for this phase. The fuel will be distributed through a tanker, duly authorized.
If on-site fuel storage is required, this will be done in a designated area built specifically for fuel
storage in compliance with applicable regulations of the competent bodies.
Chemical inputs
In the construction phase, chemical inputs that present some level of risk to the environment or
workers will be used. All will be stored as indicated in the safety sheets. By way of example,
some of the chemical inputs to be used may be:
 Paints and solvents.
 Adhesives.

 Varnishes.
 Oils, lubricants and hydraulic fluids.
 Concrete additives (small quantities).
 Chemicals for water treatment of the closed cooling circuit during commissioning.

Sewage
Wastewater from washing mixer trucks
Wastewater will be generated from the washing of the half cane of the mixer trucks (premixed
concretes). This water will be treated in order to comply with the limits of discharge to collector
of Decree 253/079 and amending for Total Suspended Solids (TSS) and pH. The treatment system
shall consist of three sequential sedimentation pools of suitable dimensions, with sufficient
flexibility, and with an acid dosing and pH control system, to ensure compliance with the
corresponding discharge limits. Whenever possible, priority will be given to the use of the
effluent generated for the manufacture of new concrete volumes or future washing tasks.
It is estimated a generation of washing water from mixer trucks of between 700 to 800 L / day.
Industrial wastewater
During the commissioning of each installed stage, the conditioning of the pipe system of the
closed circuit of cooling water is carried out. This process consists of cleaning and passivating
the metal surfaces by filling the pipe system with water and adding chemicals (see table below).
Board 4-6: Chemical treatment, commissioning water.
Product
Dosage (ppm)
Name Function
Gengard GN7004 Scale inhibitor 50
Flogard MS6201 Corrosion inhibitor 30
Spectrus NX1100 Microbiological control, biocide. 50
Spectrus BD1500 Microbiological, biodispersant control 5
Wastewater is generated during the rinsing procedure of the pipe system, for commissioning.
During this procedure, drinking water is entered into the system and the wastewater is purged.
The water that is extracted by the purge contains treatment products and dirt from the system,
presenting its highest concentration at the beginning of the procedure and decreasing
progressively, until the water that is purged presents the same characteristics as the drinking
water that enters.
31

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
Commissioning of the system can be divided into several stages by isolating parts of the cooling
system. A total generation of 840 m3 is estimated during the commissioning of stage 1, and 765
m3 for each of the remaining modules (commissioning of stages 2, 3 and 4).
Given the concentration of chemical products and their generation rate, this effluent cannot be
conducted directly to the PDLC WWTP, so it is planned to rent tanker trucks or "frac tanks" for
storage and subsequent transfer to an authorized manager.
This process is carried out only once before the commissioning of the system, for each stage,
and could be carried out again in exceptional cases, due to uncontrolled microbiological
contamination, contamination with chemicals, etc.
Domestic waste water
Wastewater assimilable to domestic wastewater is estimated at an average of 35 m3/d, with an
increase in peak reaching almost 80 m3/d. The characteristics of this will be the classic ones of
a sewage liquid assimilable to domestic.
In the workshops there will be hygienic services that will derive their effluents to an
impermeable tank, which will be emptied regularly by a duly authorized barometric truck.
Chemical toilets will be available on work fronts away from the facilities. These will comply with
a permanent maintenance system, whose emptying will be carried out through companies that
have the relevant authorizations.
Once the domestic wastewater discharge line to the PDLC WWTP is installed, part of the
wastewater assimilable to domestic corresponding to the construction phase may be
discharged, provided that the conditions imposed by PDLC are met.
Rainwater management
Among the works associated with the expansion of PDLC are the rainwater buffer lagoons and
temporary diversion ditches in the perimeter of the planned blockage, which will lead the
rainwater to these lagoons. This infrastructure is the one that will be used during the
construction phase for the rainwater management of the proposed project.
Gas and particulate matter emissions
Some activities in the construction phase can generate air emissions, specifically in particulate
matter and combustion gases from the movement of vehicles and machinery. There are two
major sources of this type of aspect: the work fronts, and the transit of trucks and machinery.
Most of the work fronts are confined to the site of the project, so the sources of particulate
matter and gases can be associated globally with that area. It is very difficult to estimate the
particulate matter and gases that can be generated. The emission of gases is related to the
consumption of fuel on site while the emission of particulate matter is related to the activities
to be carried out.

Contingencies
Oil spill and chemical inputs

5. OPERATION PHASE
5.1 NORMAL OPERATION
Overview
The operation and maintenance phase has several planned stages, as the installation of the data
center modules develops. Stage 1 considers the operation of module 1, with its attached
facilities and main facilities, plus the electrical substation. The steps that follow involve the
additional operation of the rest of the data center modules, which will be installed progressively.
For the purposes of this Project Document, the enterprise operating at its maximum
development (with all the installed power) is considered as a conservative hypothesis.
The following figure presents a general flow chart of the normal operation phase of the
enterprise and then describes the activities that will be carried out in the different components
of the enterprise in that phase, the maintenance activities and the inputs involved. The following
section describes the associated environmental aspects.
36

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
Figure 5-1: Flow diagram of normal operation phase.
37

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
Data Center
The proposed venture consists of the storage and processing of technological data of various
clients, always under the high standards of security and protection of the company.
The proposed venture will also have support facilities, where activities will be developed as work
offices, meeting rooms, food area, toilet, among other activities necessary for its proper
functioning.
Electrical and mechanical yards
Overview
Electrical power will be provided to the data centers from the power yards and to the cooling
system from the mechanical yards. The source of supply during normal operation will be the
electrical substation built on the premises and, in the event that the transmission circuits that
come from the UTE substation fail or require maintenance, there will be emergency generators.
Electrical equipment
The electrical energy sent from the electrical substation will be distributed to the data centers,
operating remotely, under a redundancy system. There will be an automatic control system that
will allow energy to be sent from the electrical substation or from the emergency generators, if
necessary.
Cooling system
The data center is cooled using air-cooled chillers, and HVAC equipment for offices, etc. A closed
water loop transfers the energy from the Servers to the Chillers.
Electrical substation
Along with the substation to be built, there will be modular rooms to incorporate the control
room and auxiliary services, in which the secondary equipment of the substation will be housed.
There will be fire protection devices installed in accordance with DNB requirements.
Maintenance
Introduction
This chapter describes the maintenance activities to be carried out on the facilities that make up
the proposed project, to ensure its proper functioning, prolong its useful life and reduce the
occurrence of failures that could generate operating downtimes, damage to people, equipment
and facilities.
Data center building (DC - 01) and associated equipment areas
General maintenance activities of the data center will be performed on a regular basis in
accordance with the maintenance standards of the project owner.
The main maintenance activities are listed below:
 General cleaning of all facilities contained in data centers.
38

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Inspection of the electrical installations of the data center building, checking of boards,
general cleaning of components and replacement of elements in poor condition or
damaged.
 General review of the battery system.
 Review of air conditioning systems, in which air filters will be washed or changed.
 Inspection of metal structures located in the data center, such as stairs, walkways,
elevators, etc.
 Review of roofs, channels and water drops, in the case of leaks, the damaged equipment
or facilities will be changed.
 Inspection of transformers, which will include review of design parameters, ventilation,
etc.
 Regular maintenance of outdoor areas of mechanical equipment.
 Regular maintenance of outdoor areas of electrical equipment.
Emergency generators
Maintenance and functional tests
The maintenance regime of the generators was definitively determined after performing air
quality models, according to emission conditions at different loads and during different periods
of time, also taking into account the requirements of the generators according to supplier.
It is defined that the maintenance of the generators will be carried out with a monthly frequency
and consists of putting them into operation for approximately 30 minutes. It is expected that
the generators will not be operated simultaneously, but will be operated one at a time, and
during daytime.
Fuel supply and distribution for emergency generators
Although normal operation does not include the continuous operation of generators, the supply
and distribution of fuel will be associated with normal operation, either associated with
replenishment due to consumption during periodic tests or replenishment after a period of
emergency operation.
The fuel supply and distribution process will be carried out in the supply area located in the
electrical and mechanical yard, separately, which will be distributed to each electric generator
through pipes, valves and pumps.
The fuel reception system will have an autonomous control, for greater safety during the
distribution process. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if necessary, the control system may be
operated by the operator in charge of the activity, from a central control panel. The autonomous
control system in conjunction with the level, temperature and alarm sensors will aim to detect
in time the maximum fuel load for each generator. Additionally, the temperature inside the fuel
tanks of each generator will be monitored, to avoid increases above the allowed values, which
could cause accidents or spills.
Once the fuel truck is installed in the unloading area, the operator in charge of the activity will
activate all safety protocols for the discharge of fuel.
It is estimated that the fuel supply will have a frequency of twice a year, and the transport of
fuel will be carried out by companies that have the required authorizations.
Auxiliary infrastructure
Maintenance activities for facilities supplementing the data centre are as follows:
 General inspection of all facilities and associated equipment.
39

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Cleaning of all facilities associated with the data center, parking lots, internal roads,
access gate, and rainwater collection, conduction and damping system.
 Inspection and repair of electrical connections if necessary.
Personnel
It is estimated a requirement of approximately 50 people for the venture in its final stage.
Approximately 40% of the staff will be associated with the operations of the venture and the
rest with support activities.
Inputs
Water
Drinking water will be required for both cooling systems and staff supply. By having closed water
circuits for the cooling system, the highest water consumption for this use will occur in its start-
up, both for the initial cleaning of the pipe system and for its filling, being insignificant the
replacement that may be necessary in subsequent instances of maintenance. A daily
consumption for the operation of the project is estimated at its maximum development of 48.1
m3/day, mainly for facility use (bathrooms, kitchen, etc.).
Chemical inputs
For the chemical treatment of cooling system water (closed circuit) biocides, corrosion inhibitors
and scale inhibitors will be used.
For the general maintenance of buildings and equipment, other chemical inputs will be required,
such as refrigerants for chillers and air conditioning equipment, oils for generators, anticorrosive
paint, among other inputs of general use in maintenance activities. In addition, there will be
chemical inputs associated with the cleaning of buildings and staff hygiene.
Of all the chemical inputs mentioned, those that have hazardous characteristics will be stored
in the chemical input warehouses, located in the data center building.

enterprise are detailed below.

Sewage
Domestic waste water
The wastewater generated in the services destined for the use of the personnel of the enterprise
will be collected from the generation points and sent to a pumping station within the property,
from which it will be sent to the PDLC WWTP. It is estimated a generation of approximately 2
m3 / hour.
Industrial wastewater
Industrial wastewater is generated only by controlled maintenance or breakage. They will
correspond to wastewater from washing, purges of the closed cycle cooling system during
maintenance tasks or filter rejections, and also to leaks due to valve breaks, seals etc.
Under normal operation zero generation of industrial wastewater is estimated (without
maintenance tasks or breakages). However, it is estimated a maximum generation of 4 m3/day
during occasional scheduled maintenance instances, and a generation of 38 L/min for a
maximum of 8 h, in case of breakages.
These waters will be drained into a sump tank and removed and properly disposed of by
managers authorized by DINACEA.
Rainwater management
As mentioned in Section 3.2, the project will have a rainwater collection and conduction network
that will drain into two buffer lagoons within the property, which, in turn, will discharge into a
glen in one case, and into an existing culvert in the other.

Next, the sound pressure levels of the equipment that make up the facilities of the proposed
undertaking are presented.
Table 3- 1: Sound emission sources during normal operation.
Fountain Source Emission NPS (dBA)
Generators (EYD and MYD) 92
AHU (SLTE) 60
AHU (DC, facilities service area) 60
"Chiller" 104
Capacitors 67
Electrical substation transformer 80
Load Bank 98
Within normal operation, two possible scenarios are also distinguished:
1. Permanent normal operation.
2. Normal operation during generator maintenance.
The permanent normal operation scenario contemplates the activities and processes that are
carried out continuously, leaving aside specific activities such as generator maintenance. This
operation scenario will be the one that is present the vast majority of the time. The most
relevant emission sources in this scenario will be those indicated in the previous list, except for
generators and the load bank.
46

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
The normal operation scenario during maintenance of generators will be given a percentage of
the time extremely limited, and will contemplate, in addition to the operation of the equipment
mentioned for normal operation without maintenance, the operation of one generator at a time
for a total of 6 hours per year each, always during daytime, and the load bank.
Induced transit
The operation of the proposed venture will require both the entry of materials into the property,
as well as the exit of waste from the property to the final disposal sites, as indicated in the
following table.
Board 5-4: Transit of induced cargo in operation.
Type of vehicle Load Quantity (vehicles/month)
Waste category II 4
Simple truck
Waste category I 1
Tanker Fuel 0,3 1
Simple truck Spare parts 5
The traffic that is estimated to be generated during the operation for the transport of personnel,
based on the implementation of a mobility survey and assuming that the proportions are
maintained, would be 18 cars, 6 motorcycles and 4 bicycles, during the hours of entry and exit
of the main shift. These numbers are estimates and may vary during the development of the
project.
Contingencies
Introduction
A Contingency and Emergency Prevention Plan of the proposed enterprise will be developed
where the possible risks associated with the operation of the proposed venture will be identified
and responsibilities will be established.
The main contingencies identified are described below, as well as the prevention measures that
will be taken in advance and the action measures in case they occur.
Igneous focus
The proposed project will have in all its facilities and buildings a wet and dry network for fire
control, taps and extinguishers type ABC with adequate capacity for installation. The
corresponding permit will be processed with DNB.
As preventive measures, seeking to avoid emergency situations, there are the following:
 Safe system operation procedures.
 Permanent training of the personnel in charge.
 Installation of signage in accordance with current regulations.
 Scheduling of revisions and preventive maintenance of equipment and infrastructure.
 Use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) when required by the tasks they perform.
 Maintain areas with fire risk demarcated, marked and with indications.
1 This represents 4 trucks per year, considering trucks of 25 m3 volume.
47

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
 Establish and demarcate security zones.
 Conduct drills.
 Conduct training in fire control, use and handling of fire extinguishers.
Oil spills and chemical inputs
The proposed venture will incorporate measures to prevent the spillage of chemical inputs and
hydrocarbons. The main measures include the design of tanks, pipes and storage facilities for
chemical inputs and fuels with high safety standards and in compliance with the respective
regulations. In addition, periodic controls will be carried out on facilities and equipment
intended to store hazardous substances.
The areas destined for the loading of fuel for mechanical and electrical yards will have a spill
containment system. Each system will have a parapet and a spill containment chamber with leak
detection sensors. If a fuel spill occurs, the liquids will be pumped into a tanker truck.
For fuel distribution systems, a containment system consisting of double-walled pipes is
contemplated that will lead the potential spill by gravity to the spill containment chamber of the
fuel loading area.
The generator sheds will house the generators and the fuel tanks, which have a double-wall
containment system of adequate capacity and a leak detection system.
In the event of an eventual contact of rainwater with the hydrocarbons entering the spill
containment chambers in the fuel loading areas, an alarm will be activated. Additionally, oil and
hydrocarbon separation chambers will be considered, whose function allows that, in case of
heavy rains, water can be discharged into the sewer without the presence of oil or fuel.
All hazardous waste that may be generated in this process will be handled by properly trained
personnel, then transported and disposed of by duly authorized companies.
In those episodes in which the infrastructure planned for the containment of spills is not
sufficient, action will be taken in accordance with a specific procedure, which will be detailed in
the Contingency and Emergency Prevention Plan.
The areas destined for the loading of chemical inputs will have the containment infrastructure
required in the event of spills during the loading operation.
5.3 EMERGENCY OPERATION
Overview
Emergency operation is considered to be that which occurs during a power failure either from
the substation or from the distribution network.
Emergency generators, already mentioned in Section 3.2.5, will provide electrical power to the
data centers in the event of supply failures.
In terms of the Environmental Impact Study, only the event of total power supply failure is
considered, in which case all emergency generators will operate simultaneously, without
prejudice to the fact that there may also be minor or sectorized electrical failure events in which
fewer emergency generators operate simultaneously. This is considered a conservative scenario
for the purposes of such a study.
48

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
In the Figure 5-4 A general flow diagram of the emergency operation phase is presented and the
activities associated with the emergency generators are described below. The following section
describes the associated environmental aspects.
Emergency generators
The power yards will deliver the energy support from the emergency generators to the data
center buildings, in case of contingency.
The emergency generators will operate independently in each power yard, so that only the
generators associated with the affected areas start operating. In the event of a total power
outage, an alarm will be triggered, along with all emergency generators, which will start
operating to ensure the operational continuity of the data centers, including their support
facilities such as luminaires, control rooms and telecommunications.
The generators will work on diesel fuel and will have a storage tank attached to the bottom of
each equipment, so it is an integral part of it. Each fuel tank shall be sized so that it can contain
at least the volume of fuel that a generator would use in 24 hours.
49

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
Figure 5-4: Emergency Operation Phase Flowchart
50

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
5.4 ENVIRONMENTAL ASPECTS OF EMERGENCY OPERATION
Introduction
This section identifies and characterizes the environmental aspects associated with the activities
of the proposed enterprise in emergency operation. Emissions differ in quality or quantity from
those identified for the normal operation phase.
Identification of environmental aspects
Only in the event of a power outage and for a limited period of time could the operation of
emergency generators trigger the simultaneous operation of some or all generators, and
therefore the generation of noise emissions and flue gases will be substantially different from
that existing during the normal operation phase.
Board 5-5: Environmental aspects associated with the emergency operation phase of the proposed venture.
Phase Typology Aspect
Noise emissions in emergency operation
EO Air emissions
Emission of flue gases in emergency operation
Characterization of environmental aspects
Gaseous emissions
During emergency operation, the generators could operate simultaneously, making them the
most relevant sources of flue gas emissions.
Although for the purposes of the environmental impact study in the emergency operation the
total failure of the electrical system is considered, it is reiterated that the total failure event is
unlikely, there may be minor or sectorized electrical failure events, in which fewer emergency
generators operate simultaneously.
It should be noted that the generators to be used will have certification of emission levels "Tier
2" from the US EPA.
Emergency operation events are expected to be short-lived, taking into account the historical
service data provided by UTE and its guaranteed service level. At first they can occur both during
the day and at night.
The following table presents the emission rates that were used in the modeling of air quality
during emergency operation, of CO, NO2, PM10, PM2.5 and SO2 for each type of generator,
during its emergency operation.
Board 5-6: Emission rates by source and parameter for emergency operation.

Nox 1 hour g/s 7,55 1,96

CO 8 hours g/s 0,44 1,06

Noise emissions
During emergency operation the most relevant noise sources will be the generators in
operation, which will operate simultaneously. The characterization of noise emissions is that
already presented for normal operation.
As mentioned above, emergency generators are expected to operate for short periods of time.
52

PROJECT DOCUMENT – TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.
6. CLOSING PHASE
6.1 OVERVIEW
The operation of the proposed project does not have a defined end date; It is anticipated that
services will be offered on an ongoing basis and site equipment and infrastructure will be
upgraded and renewed as necessary.
If the holder decides to leave PDLC, the steps required for the cessation of operations and
closure of the site will be executed. These steps may vary according to the reason for closure,
and may include from dismantling all buildings, equipment and infrastructure to only the
execution of the necessary works to eliminate all confidential information and technology, if a
future user wishes to retain the infrastructure. All works will be executed in compliance with
national and municipal regulations and applicable environmental policies.
6.2 ENVIRONMENTAL ASPECTS
The environmental aspects during the decommissioning phase are mainly associated with the
generation of waste, due to the dismantling of equipment and demolition of civil works, traffic
induced by the removal of said waste and transport of required personnel. On the other hand,
there could be contingencies associated with the dismantling of fuel tanks and chemical inputs.
Board 6-1: Environmental aspects associated with the closure phase of the proposed venture.
Phase Typology Aspect
Generation of waste assimilable to domestic
Solid waste ROC Generation
Generation of special waste
CL Transit Induced transit
Oil spill
Contingencies Spillage of chemical inputs
Igneous focus

documentos.nombre: Estudio de Impacto Ambiental - Proyecto Teros
documentos.resumen: Estudio de Impacto Ambiental completo del proyecto de datacenter. Analiza impactos sobre calidad de aire, recursos hídricos, suelo, flora y fauna, ruido, paisaje y medio socioeconómico. Incluye modelación de dispersión atmosférica y evaluación de riesgos.

2. METODOLOGÍA DE LA EVALUACIÓN AMBIENTAL

2.1 PRINCIPIOS BÁSICOS DEL EsIA

Se entiende por Estudio de Impacto Ambiental a la aplicación de un procedimiento técnico de
análisis de un emprendimiento que permite la identificación, predicción y evaluación de las
posibles consecuencias ambientales que se puedan producir durante su ejecución. Un EsIA debe
incluir las medidas de mitigación que deben adoptarse a fin de reducir, eliminar o compensar
los impactos ambientales negativos que puedan producirse.

Se entiende por medio ambiente al sistema global constituido por los elementos naturales y
artificiales de naturaleza física, biológica y sociocultural, y por sus interrelaciones que rigen y
condicionan la vida en todas sus manifestaciones.

Se entiende por impacto ambiental a toda modificación de cualquier factor ambiental del
sistema medio ambiente o de alguna de sus relaciones producida por una acción, actividad o
aspecto de un emprendimiento en cualquiera de sus fases: construcción, operación o clausura.

Sobre la base de estas definiciones, el objetivo del EslA es la identificación de todos los impactos,
producidos directa o indirectamente por el emprendimiento, y la evaluación de los impactos
negativos que se hayan considerado significativos.

El proceso metodológico de un EslA busca alcanzar en la forma más sistemática y objetiva
posible, la identificación de estos impactos significativos, su predicción y cuantificación, así como
la determinación del grado de riesgo que pueden implicar.

No siempre es posible una cuantificación de los impactos identificados, ya que la predicción de
los mismos se encuentra condicionada por cuatro aspectos:

O Carencia de información suficiente sobre algunos de los componentes del medio
ambiente que puedan ser fundamentales.

O Ausencia de un adecuado conocimiento de la respuesta de muchos componentes del
sistema biológico y social frente a una acción determinada.

O Modificaciones que sufre un proyecto en su versión original al momento de su
ejecución.

O No determinación a priori de aspectos durante la fase de construcción, que derivaran
de decisiones que se tomen durante el avance de las obras.

Por lo tanto, y puesto que se ha visto que en la mayoría de los casos sólo un conjunto pequeño
de impactos son los responsables de las mayores afectaciones ambientales, y que la reducción
o mitigación de estos implicaría la minimización de las consecuencias ambientales del
emprendimiento hasta niveles admisibles, es sobre este conjunto que se centra la profundidad
de los análisis del EslA.

2.2 METODOLOGÍA
2.2.1 Introducción

Para la evaluación ambiental se parte del emprendimiento propuesto, considerando cada una
de sus fases. A partir del análisis de dicho emprendimiento y de la caracterización del medio
receptor en el cual se insertará, se utiliza una metodología clásica en cuanto a identificación,
valoración y evaluación de impactos ambientales. Para ello se aplica una técnica matricial
siguiendo los pasos que se describen a continuación.

Gia) estudio ingeniería ambiental 11
w

ESTUDIO DE IMPACTO AMBIENTAL — PROYECTO TEROS ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

2.2.2 Identificación de impactos

En primer lugar, se procede a la identificación de los principales aspectos ambientales derivados
de las actividades requeridas para el desarrollo de cada fase del emprendimiento. Se entiende
por aspecto ambiental cualquier elemento o característica que derive de una actividad del
emprendimiento, o de cualquier sustancia o producto utilizado o generado por éste, que pueda
producir impactos ambientales.

Luego, mediante la utilización de una matriz de interacción, se pasa a la identificación de los
impactos potenciales derivados de la interacción de los aspectos ambientales con los factores
ambientales, para pasar a su valoración. Como la metodología es predictiva, todos los impactos
son potenciales.

2.2.3 Valoración de impactos

La valoración de impactos es una etapa cualitativa que permite medir la significancia de estos.
Dado el conocimiento con el que se cuenta para las afectaciones de esta índole, la valoración se
hace en base a las variables y criterios presentados en la Tabla 2-1.

En función de los valores asignados a las variables consideradas, se determina la significancia de
cada impacto como la suma de estos valores. En base a lo anterior, se tiene tres tipos de
impactos, y para cada uno de estos tipos se plantea una acción en cuanto a su evaluación, según
se resumen en la Tabla 2-2.

El valor de significancia resultante del proceso de valoración no corresponde al riesgo o
magnitud esperada del efecto de un impacto ambiental potencial, sino que representa el nivel
de interés de ese impacto potencial a los efectos del proceso de evaluación. Para ejemplificar, a
un impacto al cual se le asigna una significancia alta en el paso de valoración, puede mostrarse
que tiene un efecto ambiental de poca relevancia en el paso de modelación y evaluación, sin
embargo, estos últimos dos pasos son necesarios para llegar a esa conclusión.

2.2.4 Evaluación de impactos

Los impactos de significancia alta son evaluados, como también los de significancia media que
así lo requieran, comparando con algún criterio que permita definir su admisibilidad o la
necesidad de establecer medidas de mitigación adicionales a fin de reducir sus efectos sobre los
factores ambientales impactados.

Esto implica la utilización de modelos físicos, matemáticos, computacionales o conceptuales que
permitan una adecuada cuantificación de la afectación potencial del impacto. De esta evaluación
surge o no la necesidad de implementar medidas sobre el impacto (gestión, mitigación o
compensación), y la necesidad de evaluar los posibles impactos residuales, previo a la
conclusión. En estos casos se explican los modelos utilizados y las medidas que se incorporan.

3. CARACTERIZACIÓN DEL MEDIO RECEPTOR
3.1 MEDIO FÍSICO
3.1.1 Clima

De acuerdo con la clasificación climática de Kóppen, Uruguay es un país templado y húmedo
(tipo “C”), con precipitaciones todo el año (tipo “f”) y con una temperatura en el mes más cálido
superior a los 22°C (tipo “a”). Por tal motivo, le corresponde la clasificación “Cfa”. Dadas las
características orográficas del país, no existen barreras que afecten la distribución de la
temperatura y precipitaciones. Predominan las formas bajas, por lo que las variaciones
horizontales son pequeñas y no se identifican distintos tipos de clima.

De acuerdo con los datos de la estación meteorológica de Carrasco -estación más cercana al
área en estudio-, la temperatura media anual en la zona es de 16,82C. Por su parte, la
temperatura máxima media en la región es de 21,1%C y la temperatura mínima media es de
12,42C.

Las precipitaciones medias anuales son de 1.163 mm. No existe una estación lluviosa típica,
aunque en primavera y verano se registran volúmenes algo mayores que en el resto del año. Si
bien el promedio de días con precipitaciones al año es de 84, existen variaciones interanuales.

Las medias anuales mencionadas se obtienen de datos proporcionados por el INUMET para el
período 1991-2020. Se presentan en la siguiente tabla las medias mensuales correspondientes
a cada parámetro.

máxima media (2C); TNM - Temperatura mínima media (2C); RR - Precipitación acumulada (mm); FRR - Días con

3.1.2 Geología
De acuerdo con la Carta Geológica del Uruguay a escala 1:500.000 (Preciozzi et al., 1985), en el
área afectada por el emprendimiento aflora una única unidad geológica, del Pleistoceno.

Dicha unidad corresponde a la Formación Libertad, caracterizada por la presencia de lodolitas,
loess y fangolitas con porcentaje variable de arenas y arcillas, de color pardo a pardo-rojizo.

En la siguiente figura se muestran las unidades geológicas presentes en el entorno del proyecto.

3.1.3 Suelos
Grupos de suelos CONEAT

La información de suelos recabada para el área afectada por el emprendimiento pertenece a la
descripción de los suelos según la Comisión Nacional de Estudios Agroeconómicos de la Tierra
(CONEAT).

Los grupos CONEAT no son estrictamente unidades cartográficas básicas del suelo, sino que
constituyen áreas homogéneas definidas por su capacidad productiva en términos de carne
bovina, ovina y lana en pie. Esta capacidad se expresa por un índice relativo a la capacidad
productiva media del país, a la que corresponde el índice 100.

Caracterización de campo

En el marco de los estudios de factibilidad del emprendimiento en estudio, en el mes de abril
del año 2018, la firma INSUELOS realizó una caracterización geotécnica del área afectada. Se
identificaron los diferentes estratos del subsuelo, así como la napa freática y eventual techo de
roca. Se describen a continuación los hallazgos de este estudio que resultan relevantes a los
efectos de la caracterización de suelos.

Se realizaron ocho perforaciones, cuyas ubicaciones se presentan en la siguiente figura. Las
mismas se ejecutaron con un equipo rotativo manual y mecanizado, incluyendo ensayos SPT y
recolección de muestras alteradas a cada metro de avance de la perforación.

3

ES Bar A

Figura 3-5: Ubicación de las perforaciones realizadas (Fuente: INSUELOS, 2018).

Del total de las muestras recolectadas, se escogió un número representativo para los análisis de
laboratorio. Las muestras procesadas se seleccionaron de forma tal que fuera posible
caracterizar los diferentes estratos encontrados en las perforaciones. Los estratos mencionados
se identificaron en campo a través de la descripción táctil-visual de los materiales.

Sobre las muestras seleccionadas se ejecutaron los siguientes ensayos: determinación de
humedad natural, análisis granulométrico y determinación de límites de Atterberg (límite líquido
y límite plástico). A partir de la información obtenida del análisis granulométrico y las
determinaciones de límites de Atterberg, se realizó la clasificación de cada muestra procesada
mediante el Sistema Unificado de Clasificación de Suelos (SUCS).

El perfil de suelo se caracteriza por una capa superior de suelo orgánico negro cuyo espesor varía
entre 0,50 m (B03) y 1,50 m (B08), seguida por una alternancia de estratos arcillosos de
coloraciones variadas (marrón, gris, gris verdoso), de consistencias variables en profundidad y
desde blandas a muy duras según la ubicación en el predio. Los estratos arcillosos presentan en
todos los puntos contenidos variables de carbonato de calcio.

En todos los puntos se alcanzó la condición de rechazo en los ensayos SPT, a profundidades que
oscilan entre los 9 y 13 m.

En la perforación BO3, entre los 13 y los 15 m de profundidad, se determinó la presencia de roca
completamente meteorizada, identificando clastos de roca en una matriz arcillosa.

Con respecto a la plasticidad de los suelos, 50 muestras de las 85 analizadas resultan clasificadas
como arcillas de alta compresibilidad SUCS, presentando plasticidades altas a muy altas. Estos
suelos suelen presentar potencial expansivo alto, caracterizándose por la ocurrencia de fuertes
procesos de contracción-retracción frente a cambios importantes en su contenido de humedad.

3.1.5 Calidad de aire

La presente caracterización de la calidad del aire se basa en el análisis realizado de los datos de
estaciones de monitoreo, que se presenta en el ANEXO I.

La estación de monitoreo de calidad de aire más cercana al sitio de implantación del
emprendimiento corresponde a la estación “Barradas”, perteneciente a la DINACEA, la cual se
encuentra ubicada en las cercanías de la Ruta Interbalnearia y la calle Barradas, a unos 7,7 km
de distancia del emprendimiento propuesto. Dado que ésta no cuenta con datos completos para
todos los parámetros de interés, se consideraron también otras estaciones que se entienden
representativas, a saber: “Tres Cruces”, “Ciudad Vieja” y “Curva de Maroñas”, pertenecientes a
la red de monitoreo de calidad de aire de la Intendencia de Montevideo.

Para cada parámetro y estación se analizó la completitud de los datos, de modo de determinar
su representatividad. De este análisis se tiene que únicamente las estaciones de Tres Cruces y
Curva de Maroñas cuentan con años con más de 75 % de datos registrados para NO,. En el caso
del PM», únicamente la estación de Ciudad Vieja cuenta con varios años con más de un 75 %
de datos registrados. Para PM1o la estación de Tres Cruces presenta años que cumplen con el
criterio de completitud, y para el SO», la estación de Barradas presenta un año con más del 75 %
de datos. Además, sólo en el caso de PM,s se cuenta con tres años de datos completos, como
era requerido. Para NO», PM10 y SO, se utilizan los datos de los años que cumplen con el criterio
de completitud, aunque sean menos de tres. Como regla general, para todos los enfoques se
utilizan los mejores datos en términos de completitud.

En líneas generales, para el cálculo de promedios horarios se considera el percentil 0,90 de los
valores horarios de cada año de datos completos. Si hay más de un año con datos completos, se
toma el máximo, a modo conservador. Para el cálculo de promedios diarios se considera el
percentil 0,90 de los promedios diarios para cada año completo y luego, si se cuenta con más de
un año de datos completos, se toma el promedio de estos valores. Para la caracterización de la

3.2 MEDIO BIÓTICO
3.2.1 Biota terrestre
Prioridades de conservación dentro de las eco-regiones de Uruguay

En el informe “Prioridades de conservación dentro de las eco-regiones de Uruguay” (Brazeiro y
Soutullo, 2012), se identifican los sitios prioritarios para la conservación dentro de cada una de
las eco-regiones de Uruguay.

Los criterios de priorización de las áreas para la conservación de la biodiversidad se
establecieron tomando en cuenta dos importantes antecedentes, en los cuales se definen los
objetivos nacionales vinculados a la conservación de la biodiversidad: la Estrategia Nacional de
Biodiversidad, que responde a un compromiso asumido por el país ante la Convención sobre
Diversidad Biológica (ratificada en Uruguay por Ley 16.408 del 27 de agosto de 1993) y la Ley
17.234 del 22 de febrero de 2000, de creación del Sistema Nacional de Áreas Protegidas (SNAP).

A partir de estos antecedentes se establecieron los siguientes criterios de priorización: 1) riqueza
de especies; 2) servicios ecosistémicos; 3) ecosistemas amenazados. Los criterios fueron
evaluados en forma binaria, asignándose el valor “1” si se cumple satisfactoriamente (según el
criterio que se detalla en cada caso), o “0” si no se satisface. En base a estos criterios se
identificaron y mapearon con alta resolución espacial, ecosistemas de máxima y alta prioridad
por eco-región, abarcando en total un 12,2% del territorio nacional.

En la Figura 3-9 se presenta el mapa de prioridades de conservación obtenido para la eco-región
Graven de Santa Lucía. Un 15,3% de la eco-región fue identificado como máxima y alta prioridad
(2 y 3 criterios). El emprendimiento se ubica en una zona de baja prioridad por no cumplir con
ninguna de las condiciones establecidas como criterio de priorización.

Caracterización ambiental del entorno
Introducción

En el marco de los estudios de factibilidad de este emprendimiento, se realizó una
caracterización biótica ambiental del entorno del proyecto utilizando sistemas de información
geográfica (SIG), a cargo de TETRA Consultores.

Para esta caracterización se consideraron dos enfoques: componentes ambientales o de hábitat
presentes en un radio de aproximadamente 10km de distancia al área del proyecto, y
antecedentes de estudios de fauna presente dentro del mismo, principalmente aves. A
continuación, se presenta esta caracterización.

En el entorno próximo al área de estudio se pueden encontrar diversos componentes
ambientales, tales como cultivos y centros urbanos, pero también parches naturales: praderas,
montes, etc., como puede verse en la Figura 3-10. El mapa de base de usos de hábitat es el
disponible de FAO-DINAMA (2015).

A su vez, para el análisis de especies potenciales fueron incluidos diversos listados de registros
de aves que se obtuvieron a partir de la base de datos de la plataforma eBird (años 2015 y 2016).

Caracterización ambiental del predio en estudio

El proyecto se implantará sobre un predio de la Zona Franca de Parque de las Ciencias, la cual
será ampliada para incluir los predios del proyecto. Esta ampliación ya cuenta con autorización
de la DINACEA (Resolución Ministerial 269/2020, Ministerio de Ambiente). Parte de las
actividades de la implementación de la Zona Franca comprenden el movimiento de suelos,
nivelación del predio y construcción de caminos perimetrales internos. Estas actividades
removerán la vegetación presente, alterando el medio biótico. A lo que a los efectos del
proyecto Teros, el centro de datos comenzará su construcción sobre un predio con un medio
biótico prácticamente inexistente.

Igualmente, en el marco de los estudios de factibilidad de este emprendimiento, el día 23 de
marzo de 2018 se realizó una campaña de campo a cargo de técnicos de TETRA Consultores, con
el fin de obtener una visión directa y con información primaria acerca de los valores ecológicos
del área de análisis.

El predio del proyecto propuesto, a la fecha de la campaña solo se lo puede reconocer como un
predio de cultivo abandonado, encontrándose vegetación propia de ambientes naturalizados
que no llegan a recuperarse en forma de pradera natural, sino que presentan elementos de flora
típicos de ambientes degradados por actividades antrópicas. Este tipo de ambiente naturalizado
es el que se menciona que se encuentra por varios puntos en el entorno del proyecto.

La mayor superficie del predio se encuentra cubierta por pastizales, seguido por pastizales-
pajonales húmedos, siendo los demás componentes identificados menos frecuentes y puntuales
en el área.
