3.3 PRESENTACIÓN DEL PROYECTO A ENTREVISTADOS

A continuación, se presenta la información aportada a los entrevistados sobre la descripción del
proyecto y los impactos socioambientales identificados, junto a sus medidas de mitigación y
compensación.

“El proyecto Teros propuesto consta de la construcción y operación de dos edificios de centros de
datos en un predio de Parque de las Ciencias, en el departamento de Canelones, en Ruta 101. El
objetivo del emprendimiento es proveer de servicios de internet a varios clientes de Latinoamérica y
el mundo.

El proyecto se desarrollaría a través de una fase de construcción y otra de operación y
mantenimiento.

Características del proyecto

El emprendimiento estaría ubicado en un predio de Parque de las Ciencias y ocupará un total de 32,5
hectáreas.

La fase de construcción se ejecutaría en dos etapas. La primera contempla la construcción del primer
edificio de centro de datos, junto con la infraestructura necesaria para su operación, una subestación
eléctrica y un edificio administrativo. En una segunda etapa, se construiría el segundo centro de
datos y la infraestructura asociada al mismo. La duración de la primera etapa de obra sería de entre
36 y 48 meses y se estima que crearía entre 500 y 600 puestos de trabajo, con picos de
aproximadamente 1.200*. La segunda etapa se ejecutaría en algún momento luego de la primera
etapa, cuando así lo disponga el titular.

La fase de operación consiste en el funcionamiento del edificio de centro de datos, y en el
almacenamiento y procesamiento en él de los datos tecnológicos de varios clientes. Durante esa fase
también funcionarán las oficinas de trabajo, salas comunes, comedor, entre otras, que estarán
ubicadas en el edificio de administración. La principal fuente de electricidad del emprendimiento será
la subestación eléctrica y, en el caso, poco probable, de corte de energía, se encenderían generadores
eléctricos de emergencia. Durante la fase de operación, con el proyecto en su fase final totalmente
desarrollada y operando, se estima que el emprendimiento crearía entre 200 y 250 puestos de
trabajo.

4. MEDIO ANTRÓPICO

El medio antrópico es el espacio físico, social y cultural sobre el que tendrán incidencia los impactos
generados por un emprendimiento determinado. El estudio del medio antrópico, y la definición del
área de influencia de un emprendimiento, permiten comprender el territorio en el que un proyecto
se desarrollará.

4.1 DEFINICIÓN DEL ÁREA DE INFLUENCIA

El área de influencia es el área física sobre la que un proyecto produce impactos (sociales,
económicos, ambientales) causados por el desarrollo de sus actividades asociadas.

No solo comprende a la superficie de tierra en la que está inmerso el proyecto, sino también a todos
los grupos sociales de individuos, comunidades, empresas, organizaciones y entidades de gobierno
que se relacionan.

En otras palabras, un área de influencia se compone de las personas que se verán potencialmente
afectadas por el impacto de un proyecto, tanto en las “comunidades de lugar” como en las
“comunidades de interés”.

Según define la Corporación Financiera Internacional (IFC), el área de influencia es el área que
posiblemente pueda verse afectada por los impactos acumulativos y el desarrollo planificado del
proyecto, y las áreas posiblemente afectadas por impactos en el desarrollo que, aunque no hayan
sido planeados, puedan ser predecibles a causa del proyecto y que puedan ocurrir posteriormente.

La Norma de Desempeño N°1 de la IFC señala que el área de influencia de un proyecto comprende,
cuando sea pertinente:

1) el (los) sitio(s) primario(s) del proyecto y las instalaciones relacionadas que el cliente
(incluyendo a sus contratistas) desarrolle y controle, tales como los corredores para la
transmisión de energía, tuberías, canales, túneles, caminos de reubicación y acceso, área de
transición o eliminación, campos de construcción;

Il) instalaciones conexas que no estén financiadas como parte del proyecto (el cliente o terceros,
incluyendo el gobierno, pueden proveer la financiación de forma separada) y cuya viabilidad y
existencia dependan de manera exclusiva del proyecto y cuyos bienes o servicios sean
esenciales para la operación satisfactoria del proyecto;

Ill) área que posiblemente puedan ser afectada por los impactos acumulativos por el desarrollo
planificado adicional del proyecto, cualquier condición o proyecto existente y otros
desarrollos asociados al proyecto que estén identificados de manera realista al momento de la
realización de la Evaluación Social y Ambiental;

IV) áreas posiblemente afectadas por impactos en el desarrollo que, aunque no hayan sido
planeados, puedan ser predecibles a causa del proyecto y que puedan ocurrir posteriormente
o en una ubicación diferente.

Tomando estos lineamientos, el área de influencia del proyecto Teros queda delimitada por Camino
de los Horneros al Oeste, arroyo Carrasco al Este, Ruta 102 al Norte y avenida a la Rambla Costanera
al Sur.

13

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

Las áreas urbanas del municipio incluidas en el área de influencia son el municipio de Nicolich-
Ciudad Líber Seregni, y sus barrios -que conforman la microrregión influenciada- Colonia Nicolich,
Aeroparque, Santa Teresita y el barrio privado Colinas de Carrasco.*

El área de influencia del proyecto Teros se encuentra en una zona suburbana del departamento de
Canelones que presenta desde hace años un importante dinamismo, con proyección de varios
emprendimientos industriales y logísticos.

Está caracterizada por el eje de las rutas 101 y 102, que sirven de corredores a la salida Este de
Montevideo y conectan con la Ruta Interbalnearia y la Ruta 8, generando una zona particular que va
entre los arroyos Carrasco y Pando, y que incluye varios centros poblados.

4.2 ORIGEN Y DESARROLLO DEL ÁREA DE INFLUENCIA

La zona que hoy forma parte del municipio Nicolich-Ciudad Líber Seregni permaneció despoblada
hasta el primer cuarto del siglo pasado. Los primeros pobladores aparecieron en 1925, cuando un
privado compró un campo de 550 hectáreas (lo que actualmente es el barrio Colonia Nicolich) y lo
fraccionó en chacras.®

Es así como los primeros residentes fueron los medianeros y arrendatarios de esas chacras,
orientales e inmigrantes europeos que fueron conformando una zona agrícola, con chacras y
huertas, que abastecían a la creciente ciudad de Montevideo.

En la década del 40, época en la que se comienza a construir el Aeropuerto Internacional de
Carrasco, la zona comenzó a vivir otro cambio demográfico.

La zona comenzó a tener una urbanización con baja densidad poblacional y con familias que
habitaban un territorio sin servicios básicos. Los trabajadores de las industrias de Paso Carrasco -
ubicado a ocho kilómetros de Colonia Nicolich- y del aeropuerto, le fueron dando un perfil más
urbano a la localidad, que paralelamente se consolidaba también con una población dedicada a la
agricultura intensiva.

Según diferentes informaciones oficiales de la Intendencia de Canelones, este proceso de
crecimiento demográfico no fue acompañado con un desarrollo de infraestructura y servicios, lo que
generó una creciente población que demandaba pero no encontraba servicios.

En los años sesenta se fraccionaron otros predios de la zona y se dio nacimiento a Colonia Lamas, y
los barrios Santa Teresita y Villa Aeroparque, recibiendo un perfil de personas jóvenes que llegaban
a la zona en busca de mejores condiciones habitacionales.

Las diferentes crisis económicas que atravesó el país durante la década del 90 y el comienzo del siglo
XXI, hicieron que la zona recibiera una oleada masiva de nuevos pobladores, expulsada de la zona
metropolitana. Esta nueva población llegó desde Montevideo y ocupó terrenos privados
abandonados, mientras paralelamente se afianzaba una nueva modalidad de urbanización: los
barrios privados.

5 El barrio privado Las Higueritas, ubicado sobre la calle Gonzalo Rodríguez, está incluido administrativamente,
y a los efectos de este trabajo, dentro del barrio Santa Teresita.

QUIRINO

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

Desde hace casi dos décadas, la zona atraviesa nuevos procesos de cambios económicos, sociales,
culturales y demográficos, que tienen que ver con la coexistencia y convivencia de múltiples
identidades, diferentes territorios y grupos sociales que habitan el lugar, y con el potencial que tiene
la zona para poder constituirse como un territorio logístico e industrial.

La captación de inversiones y la localización de un conjunto de emprendimientos cambiaron el
paisaje y reconfiguraron el entramado social, el económico y el paisaje.

Los grandes emprendimientos de actividades comerciales, industriales y logísticas, situados en los
corredores de las rutas 101 y 102, conformaron un nuevo paisaje productivo que lograron constituir
a la zona como un enclave industrial-logístico y de servicios, pasando de tener componentes
clásicamente agrarios a tener características más industriales, de servicio, deportivos, recreativos y
residenciales.

Esta serie de transformaciones conviven con un escenario de pobreza, exclusión, precariedad
urbana, habitacional y de déficits urbanos, vinculados con las condiciones de vida de la población:
infraestructura, equipamiento, accesibilidad y conectividad.

Los gobiernos locales y departamentales fueron teniendo un mayor acercamiento al territorio con la
creación de la Junta Local de Colonia Nicolich, en 2006, y con la creación del Municipio en 2010.
Estos cambios administrativos posibilitaron el inicio de la planificación urbana, a través del gobierno
local y la participación ciudadana en las decisiones locales, y reforzaron la institucionalidad, la
participación ciudadana y la capacidad de gobierno.

4.3 PRESENCIA INSTITUCIONAL Y VIDA COTIDIANA

A pesar de su variante historia, Nicolich recién tuvo en 2006 una Oficina Delegada, que se ocupaba
de las cuestiones de la localidad, pero que dependía administrativamente de la Junta Local de San
José de Carrasco y de la Junta Local de Pando.

Luego de ese inicio, y también en el 2006, se creó la Junta Local de Colonia Nicolich, que ordenó el
trabajo institucional y el vínculo con la comunidad. Tras cuatro años en esa modalidad, en 2010, tras
la creación de los municipios, se logró un mayor grado de descentralización política y participación
ciudadana en la zona.

Este cambio institucional y político legitimó la escala local de gestión de la localidad. El municipio de
Colonia Nicolich articula los diferentes organismos del Estado, con el objetivo de unificar los servicios
y promover la instalación de servicios ausentes en el territorio, además de fortalecer el entramado
social de la localidad.

Documentos del municipio especifican que la estrategia desplegada desde la Oficina Delegada de
Nicolich, la Junta Local y ahora el municipio, fue la de generar participación entre las diferentes
instituciones desplegadas en la localidad y la población, alcanzando niveles altos de participación y
generando acuerdos y propuestas surgidas desde lo local, como la construcción de un polo
educativo.

A nivel educativo, en el año 2005, el barrio Aeroparque -uno de los que presenta niveles más bajos
de desarrollo- tenía una oferta educativa muy acotada, y se limitaba solo a la escuela primaria N°58.

15

RIVERO
QUIRINO

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

Luego del nivel primario, los jóvenes debían viajar día a día hacia Pando, Montevideo u otras
localidades.

El desarrollo de la presencia del Estado, y la organización vecinal, lograron la compra de un predio en
el barrio para construir allí el actual polo educativo de Aeroparque, con el que se buscó centralizar
en un mismo lugar todos los niveles educativos.

Cuenta con una casa de acompañamiento, que atiende a niños desde los 45 días de nacidos hasta los
2 años. Una vez concurrido al acompañamiento, pasan al CAIF preescolar, el cual atiende a aquellos
niños, desde los 2 años hasta los 5 años.

También se construyó una nueva escuela primaria; y una UTU CEA (Centro Educativo Asociado), lo
que permite generar sinergias desde temprana edad con los alumnos de la UTU y los de la escuela.
También está en construcción, pero con una pronta inauguración, el liceo secundario, que le daría
un cierre al Polo Educativo de Educación.

Según la opinión de la comunidad, el polo educativo brinda la posibilidad de ampliar el nivel
educativo de la zona, y ofrece una amplia oferta educativa sin que los estudiantes de la localidad
tengan que trasladarse hacia otras localidades para obtener una educación formal.

4.4 ACTIVIDAD ECONÓMICA Y USO DEL SUELO

Las actividades económicas y los diferentes usos que se le dio al suelo en Colonia Nicolich variaron a
través de los años. Los primeros pobladores del lugar le dieron un perfil agrícola a la zona, que fue
variando hasta lo que predomina hoy: suelos dedicados a la logística, servicios y una consolidación
urbana de la zona.

4.5 DIMENSIONES SOCIODEMOGRÁFICAS

A continuación se presentan las características sociodemográficas del área de influencia del proyecto
propuesto, a nivel departamental y local, con datos del Censo 2011 y la Encuesta Continua de
Hogares 2019.

4.5.1 Escala departamental - Canelones

El departamento de Canelones está ubicado al Sur del país, limita al Norte con Florida, al Este con
Lavalleja y Maldonado, al Sur con el Océano Atlántico, al Sureste con Montevideo y al Oeste con San
José. En la parte Oeste y Norte el límite con los demás departamentos lo forma el río Santa Lucía, y
en la parte Este lo forman la Cuchilla Grande y el Arroyo Solís Grande.

Con 520.187 habitantes, según el Censo 2011, es el segundo departamento más poblado del país -
por detrás de Montevideo- y cuenta con 4.536 km? en extensión; su densidad poblacional es de
114,7 hab/km?, considerablemente mayor a la del total del país, que es del 18,8 hab/km?. La
evolución de la cantidad de habitantes se muestra en la siguiente tabla e ilustración.

5. IDENTIFICACIÓN, PRIORIZACIÓN Y CATEGORIZACIÓN DE ACTORES
DE INTERÉS

Una adecuada identificación de grupos de interés requiere adoptar un método sistemático, en el
área de influencia geográfica del proyecto, para saber quién se verá afectado por cuál componente
del proyecto, quién es un legítimo actor de interés y, dentro de ellos, cuáles se encuentran en
situación de desventaja o vulnerabilidad.

Además, es importante también incluir en el análisis a aquellos grupos u organizaciones que no se
ven afectados negativamente, pero cuyos “intereses” los definen como actores sociales.

Un análisis a partir de tales intereses y el levantamiento de los mapas correspondientes pueden
ayudar a entender las motivaciones de los diferentes actores y las maneras en que podrían llegar a
influir en el proyecto.

El “interés” que cada una de estas personas o grupos diferentes tiene en un proyecto es variable.

Puede haber personas directamente afectadas por los posibles efectos ambientales o sociales del
proyecto, y también están aquellas personas o grupos que podrían ejercer influencia sobre el
proyecto, tales como autoridades reguladoras, dirigentes políticos, dirigentes de organizaciones
sociales, personas vinculadas a la academia, y otras personas que participan activamente en la
comunidad local.

También hay actores que, debido a sus conocimientos o prestigio, pueden contribuir positivamente
al proyecto, por ejemplo, actuando como mediadores imparciales (Stakeholder Engagement, IFC,
2007).

Para realizar la identificación del mapa de actores de interés, se desarrollaron una serie de
actividades, con el fin de actualizar y complementar la información disponible. Esto se realizó en
base a los datos extraídos en terreno de entrevistas y encuestas, aportes de actores relevantes,
como de personas residentes en las localidades del área de influencia, entre otras.

A continuación se presenta una lista de los grupos de interés identificados, y luego se indican sus
principales características (poder y efecto). A su vez se indica cuáles de los grupos de interés serán
afectados directamente por el proyecto.

Se definieron dos parámetros para identificar y caracterizar a los grupos de interés del proyecto:

e Poder / influencia: este parámetro indica, en base a los niveles alto, medio y bajo, si la
agrupación tiene una influencia tal que pueda afectar, tanto positivamente como

negativamente, la ejecución de las obras y actividades del emprendimiento.

e Efecto / impacto: este parámetro indica cuál será la magnitud del impacto del proyecto en
cada uno de los grupos identificados (en lo positivo y en lo negativo). Se establecieron tres
niveles de magnitud: alto, cuando el efecto en los grupos afectados es considerable,
generando efectos importantes o irreversibles; medio, cuando se trata de efectos de
magnitud considerable pero que podrán ser asimilados por el grupo; y bajo, cuando el
proyecto tendrá efectos mínimos en la población y que podrán ser revertidos sin mayores

dificultades.

6. PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL

En este capítulo se presentan los resultados a los que se arribó luego de las entrevistas realizadas a
vecinos, comerciantes, productores, autoridades políticas y referentes locales del área de influencia
del proyecto Teros propuesto, sobre sus expectativas y visiones frente a la instalación del
emprendimiento en el Parque de las Ciencias. Para observar con mejor claridad la postura de los
entrevistados, se incluirán citas de las entrevistas a lo largo del informe.

6.1 ANÁLISIS DE LOS RESULTADOS
6.1.1 Nivel de conocimiento y percepción general sobre el proyecto

En su gran mayoría, los entrevistados visualizan de forma positiva la instalación de los edificios de
centro de datos en el predio de Parque de las Ciencias por parte de los impulsores del proyecto
Teros. Entre los aspectos que más se destacan están: la expectativa por la generación de mano de
obra local y de una mayor actividad comercial, el reconocimiento que puede llegar a tener la
localidad y la mejora en infraestructura para la zona; y expresaron preocupación por el tránsito y el
cuidado del medioambiente.

Los vecinos, comerciantes y referentes locales de la localidad manifiestan conocer los aspectos
relacionados con el proyecto Teros, sobre todo los vinculados con la compra de los predios en el
Parque de las Ciencias y la instalación allí de la empresa. Los medios de comunicación nacionales y
locales y las charlas informales entre vecinos fueron la principal fuente de información.

Las autoridades políticas, en tanto, expresan un mayor grado de conocimiento. Sabían del proyecto y
de su confidencialidad, y asociaron las preocupaciones a un “desconocimiento”, sobre todo en
aquellos aspectos vinculados a la participación del municipio en la gestión de los impactos en el
tránsito y la movilidad.

En los siguientes apartados se desarrollará la relación de la comunidad con los diferentes
emprendimientos logísticos y productivos de la zona, pero buena parte de los entrevistados, tanto a
nivel político como social, reconocen en la relación comunidad-municipio-empresas un elemento
clave para el desarrollo de la zona.

En cuanto a las expectativas, los entrevistados manifiestan cierta esperanza positiva en que este
emprendimiento genere puestos de trabajo para la mano de obra local. Sin embargo, todos los
entrevistados enfatizaron en que las expectativas vinculadas a este aspecto son leves, ya que existe
la experiencia de muchos de los emprendimientos industriales de la zona que se han instalado en los
últimos años, que no han dado lugar a la mano de obra local.

Los entrevistados manifiestan una posición favorable a la construcción del proyecto Teros, que daría,
según especificaron, un “posicionamiento de la localidad”, y mejoraría la infraestructura de las calles
internas de la ciudad.

33

RIVERO
QUIRINO

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

6.1.2 Temas sensibles y preocupaciones derivadas del proyecto

Los entrevistados señalan como preocupaciones a temas que ya están entre las inquietudes de la
comunidad: la movilidad y el tránsito generado durante las diferentes etapas del proyecto y el buen
manejo de los efluentes.

La zona atraviesa desde hace varios años cambios en su desarrollo productivo, que modificaron
también el paisaje y la movilidad. Los entrevistados especifican las dificultades que se generan con el
tránsito impulsado por las industrias allí instaladas, y remarcan que esa es la principal preocupación
que se tiene cuando un emprendimiento de estas características planifica instalarse en el territorio.

Los entrevistados, tanto a nivel político, social y vecinal, coinciden en que la ciudad y los diferentes
barrios tienen altos niveles de organización barrial, y el municipio funciona como aglutinador y
articulador de reclamos pero también de promovedor de instancias de organización.

Las autoridades municipales entrevistadas hacen hincapié en la relación que el municipio promueve
con las empresas instaladas, y sugieren que esa administración local, en representación de la
comunidad, sea integrada en la gestión de la movilidad y el tránsito, el “mayor problema que tiene la
ciudad”.

“Lo que más preocupa es el tránsito y la movilidad. No hemos tenido problemas con las empresas y
sus residuos, pero nos preocupa mucho, mucho, el tema tránsito. El tránsito pesado es un gran
problema para nosotros”. Entrevistado 1 (autoridad municipal).

En la comunidad hay un interés particular por el cuidado del medioambiente, y es parte de las
preocupaciones expresadas por los entrevistados, quienes señalan que no han tenido problemas con
las empresas instaladas, y que incluso se busca juntamente con ellas el cuidado medioambiental y el
mejoramiento de espacios públicos.

“Estamos trabajando en conjunto en la recuperación de una cañada contaminada con la comunidad
educativa, los adolescentes y niños y el Parque de las Ciencias.” Entrevistado 6 (referente local).

“Tenemos buen relacionamiento con las empresas. Queremos ser parte en cuanto a qué nos va a
pasar alrededor: el control de las aguas, de los residuos y de la movilidad”. Entrevistado 1 (autoridad
municipal).

Por otra parte, si bien en la comunidad hay expectativa por la generación de nuevos puestos de
trabajo para la población local, la misma está influenciada por la experiencia. Los entrevistados
enfatizan en que, cuando la zona comenzó a recibir estas nuevas empresas, las expectativas por
conseguir puestos laborales eran altas.

La expectativa fue bajando a medida que la comunidad comenzó a observar que muchas veces las
empresas ya venían con sus empleados o demandaban mano de obra calificada.

Algunos entrevistados intentan explicar esta ausencia de puestos para la población local, y apuntan
a la falta de educación y capacitación para las capacidades que demandan las nuevas industrias del
territorio.

A pesar de esas experiencias, los entrevistados señalan que la generación de puestos de trabajo,
tanto directos como indirectos, así como el movimiento comercial que se podría llegar a dar con la
instalación de este proyecto, son de los aspectos más positivos del emprendimiento.

34

RIVERO
QUIRINO

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

6.1.3 Proyección de la zona e impacto socioeconómico del proyecto

La llegada del proyecto Teros se vio en las entrevistas como un impulso a la proyección de la ciudad
a nivel departamental y nacional. Todos los entrevistados, pero especialmente los vinculados al
comercio, la educación y el gobierno local, enfatizaron en que el emprendimiento puede darle un
nuevo reconocimiento a la localidad, que pasará a albergar el proyecto en Uruguay y logrará
posicionarse de esa forma.

“Nos hace conocidos. Que el Parque de las Ciencias está en la Ciudad Líber Seregni, que el proyecto
está en la ciudad Líber Seregni, y que están todas estas empresas. Nadie daba dos pesos por esta
zona”, entrevistado uno (autoridad municipal).

“Ahora que se instala la empresa la gente va a saber dónde estamos”, entrevistada 14 (peluquera).

La comunidad valora de buena manera el desarrollo que ha tenido la zona en estos últimos veinte
años (construcciones de polos educativos, infraestructura, servicios y presencia del Estado) y lo
atribuye al nivel de organización gubernamental y social, del que forma parte.

Se entiende que el cambio productivo y la llegada de las industrias son positivos para el país y la
zona, pero aún no logran observar que eso se traduzca en puestos laborales para la población local.
De las entrevistas se desprende que la comunidad demanda capacitación para sus recursos
humanos, y que es ahí donde algunos ven la posibilidad de proyectar esta zona al futuro:
conectando las demandas de estas nuevas empresas e industrias con la formación educativa de la
zona.

La comunidad también visualiza una proyección de la zona más “verde”, con más espacios públicos,
menos basurales, con saneamiento completo y con la regularización de algunos barrios irregulares, y
observa que las nuevas generaciones tienen mayor preocupación por el cuidado del territorio, tanto
a nivel medioambiental como social y cultural.

6.2 IMPACTOS SOCIALES

A continuación, se presentan los potenciales impactos sociales identificados sobre la comunidad de
Nicolich-Ciudad Líber Seregni, que surgen de una combinación del análisis de los resultados
anteriores y del análisis de las características principales del proyecto.

El impacto social se define como aquella modificación o alteración de la forma de vida de las
personas a causa de la actividad de un proyecto; el impacto puede crear ventajas, desventajas o
ambas a la vez.

En la siguiente tabla se describen los factores sociales sobre los cuales se considera pueden ocurrir
impactos sociales.

ESTUDIO PERCEPCIÓN SOCIAL Proyecto Teros — Junio 2021

7. CONCLUSIONES

La comunidad del área de influencia valora positivamente la llegada del proyecto Teros propuesto,
visualiza un impulso en la proyección de la zona, un reconocimiento de la localidad en el
departamento y el país y plantea desafíos al momento de aprovechar la oportunidad.

La principal preocupación de la población local es la falta de trabajo. La localidad mantiene la
expectativa de que se generen puestos para la población local y que haya una dinamización de la
economía; sin embargo, y debido a experiencias anteriores con otras empresas, la expectativa de
que la fuerza de trabajo local encuentre puestos laborales está debilitada.

La comunidad demanda capacitación, y hace hincapié en que el desafío para lograr proyección es el
de vincular las demandas de las nuevas empresas con la formación académica y técnica de su
población.

La comunidad enfatiza en que los cambios que ocurrieron en la ciudad (como la construcción de una
red educativa y de infraestructura, la presencia del Estado y sus servicios) fueron impulsados por la
organización gubernamental y social, y por el diálogo que experimentan con las empresas que se
instalan en el territorio.

El fortalecimiento del diálogo entre la comunidad y las empresas es identificado como otro de los
desafíos para lograr el desarrollo de la localidad, y se repitió en las entrevistas un deseo desde la
comunidad de dialogar con la empresa encargada del proyecto.

La empresa cuenta con un equipo dedicado que trabaja en estrecha colaboración con la comunidad
local para crear una estrategia de participación que se adapte a ayudar con las necesidades
identificadas de la comunidad.

La instalación de empresas industriales, logísticas y de servicios en la zona en los últimos años,
modificaron la relación que tenían los pobladores con el tránsito. Por lo que la movilidad y el tránsito
surgieron en las entrevistas como otra de las principales preocupaciones de la comunidad,
juntamente con la demanda de cuidado de los efluentes y emisiones a la atmósfera.

Si bien los entrevistados hicieron hincapié en que no hubo problemas con otras empresas por el
cuidado ambiental, especificaron que las nuevas generaciones especialmente están promoviendo la
protección del medioambiente.

Otro de los aspectos que la comunidad observa como positivo de la llegada de este proyecto es el
impacto que pueda tener sobre la infraestructura local, como la mejora en las calles y la iluminación.

Todos los aspectos ambientales abordados por la comunidad fueron estudiados en el estudio de
impacto ambiental del proyecto propuesto, donde se definieron las medidas adecuadas, de ser
necesarias, para hacer admisibles los impactos potenciales en el ambiente.

Environmental Authorization (hereinafter AAP) from the Ministry of Environment for the Teros
Project. The report is organized into the following chapters:

1. Presentation of the venture.

Methodology of environmental assessment.
Characterization of the receiving environment.
Identification and assessment of environmental impacts.
Environmental impact assessment.

Bases of environmental management plans.
Conclusions.

1.6 LOCATION AND ACCESS

The proposed venture is planned to be located within the Science Park (hereinafter PDLC),
located on Route 101 Km 23,500, in the City of the Coast of the department of Canelones (former
7th Cadastral Section of the department of Canelones).

Within PDLC, the proposed venture is expected to be located on the property made up of
suburban registers 47,763, 47,827, 47,828, 47,829, 47,830, 47,831 and 47,832. The property
covers an area of 32.5 ha total.

It is expected that the main access to the property of the proposed project will be made from
the 46,779 register corresponding to PDLC, whose access is by Route 101. The project owner is
considering a potential alternative access by Camino Gonzalo Rodriguez and Ruta Interbalnearia
(hereinafter IB), to be confirmed later, which could be used for transit during the construction
phase and during operation. If it is finally included, at a later stage, the corresponding transit
study will be submitted to the approval of the relevant authorities (Intendancy of Canelones and
Ministry of Transport and Public Works if route IB is included).

Plate 1-1 presents the general location of the proposed undertaking in cartography of the
Military Geographic Service (hereinafter SGM), and Plate 1-2 shows the location on satellite
image of the area.

1.7 ADMINISTRATIVE BACKGROUND

As mentioned, the proposed venture is planned to be located within the Science Park, where
industries and other enterprises are housed in a free zone regime. Therefore, the administrative
background of both PDLC and the property of the proposed enterprise are described below.

On October 11, 2019, the Municipality of Canelones (hereinafter IdC) granted PDLC, Viability of
expansion of its free zone, to include in it the registers, N ° 38,309 to N ° 38,316 (this set of
registers is now unified in the register N ° 47,763), where the proposed enterprise would be
located (the original PDLC registers were the suburban registers N ° 46,778 and N ° 46,779). In
addition, within the framework of this expansion, the loT approved the PDLC proposal to
consider the Type IV area, Tolerably Noisy Area, through Resolution N19/7595 of 11/10/2019.
By ministerial resolution 269/2020 of October 20, 2020, PDLC obtained the extension of its AAP
before the Ministry of Environment with the same objectives.

On October 28, 2020, the National Directorate of the Environment (hereinafter DINAMA, now
DINACEA) issued the declaration of Environmental Viability of Location (hereinafter VAL) for

The information presented in this document, as well as in the PD, differs in some respects from

2. ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT METHODOLOGY

2.1 BASIC PRINCIPLES OF EsIA

Environmental Impact Study is understood as the application of a technical procedure for the
analysis of an undertaking that allows the identification, prediction and evaluation of the
possible environmental consequences that may occur during its execution. An EsIA should
include the mitigation measures that need to be taken in order to reduce, eliminate or
compensate for any negative environmental impacts that may occur.

Environment is understood as the global system constituted by natural and artificial elements
of a physical, biological and sociocultural nature, and by their interrelationships that govern and
condition life in all its manifestations.

Environmental impact is understood as any modification of any environmental factor of the
environmental system or any of its relationships produced by an action, activity or aspect of an
undertaking in any of its phases: construction, operation or closure.

Based on these definitions, the objective of the EsIA is the identification of all impacts, produced
directly or indirectly by the enterprise, and the evaluation of negative impacts that have been
considered significant.

The methodological process of an EsIA seeks to achieve, in the most systematic and objective
way possible, the identification of these significant impacts, their prediction and quantification,
as well as the determination of the degree of risk they may involve.

It is not always possible to quantify the identified impacts, since their prediction is conditioned
by four aspects:

O Lack of sufficient information on some of the components of the environment that may
be critical.

O Lack of adequate knowledge of the response of many components of the biological and
social system to a given action.

O Modifications that a project undergoes in its original version at the time of its execution.

O Noa priori determination of aspects during the construction phase, which will derive
from decisions taken during the progress of the works.

Therefore, and since it has been seen that in most cases only a small set of impacts are
responsible for the greatest environmental impacts, and that the reduction or mitigation of
these would imply the minimization of the environmental consequences of the undertaking to
acceptable levels, it is on this set that the depth of the EsIA analysis is focused.

2.2 METHODOLOGY
2.2.1 Introduction

For the environmental assessment, the proposed undertaking is based, considering each of its
phases. Based on the analysis of this undertaking and the characterization of the receiving
environment in which it will be inserted, a classic methodology is used in terms of identification,
assessment and evaluation of environmental impacts. For this, a matrix technique is applied
following the steps described below.

11

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STUDY — TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

2.2.2 Identification of impacts

First, we proceed to the identification of the main environmental aspects derived from the
activities required for the development of each phase of the enterprise. An environmental
aspect is understood as any element or characteristic that derives from an activity of the
enterprise, or from any substance or product used or generated by it, that may produce
environmental impacts.

Then, through the use of an interaction matrix, we move on to the identification of the potential
impacts derived from the interaction of environmental aspects with environmental factors, to
move on to their assessment. As the methodology is predictive, all impacts are potential.

2.2.3 Impact assessment

The assessment of impacts is a qualitative stage that allows measuring the significance of these.
Given the knowledge available for affectations of this nature, the assessment is made based on
the variables and criteria presented in Table 2-1.

Based on the values assigned to the variables considered, the significance of each impact is
determined as the sum of these values. Based on the above, there are three types of impacts,
and for each of these types an action is proposed in terms of their evaluation, as summarized in
Table 2-2.

The significance value resulting from the assessment process does not correspond to the
expected risk or magnitude of the effect of a potential environmental impact, but represents
the level of interest of that potential impact for the purposes of the assessment process. To
exemplify, an impact to which a high significance is assigned in the valuation step, it can be
shown that it has an environmental effect of little relevance in the modeling and evaluation step,
however, these last two steps are necessary to reach that conclusion.

3. CHARACTERIZATION OF THE RECEIVING ENVIRONMENT
3.1 PHYSICAL ENVIRONMENT
3.1.1 Climate

According to the Kóppen climate classification, Uruguay is a temperate and humid country (type
"C"), with rainfall all year round (type "f") and with a temperature in the warmest month above
222C (type "a"). For this reason, it corresponds to the classification "Cfa". Given the orographic
characteristics of the country, there are no barriers that affect the distribution of temperature
and rainfall. Low forms predominate, so horizontal variations are small and different types of
climate are not identified.

According to data from the Carrasco weather station -station closest to the area under study-,
the average annual temperature in the area is 16.82C. For its part, the average maximum
temperature in the region is 21.12C and the average minimum temperature is 12.49C.

The average annual rainfall is 1,163 mm. There is no typical rainy season, although in spring and
summer there are somewhat higher volumes than in the rest of the year. Although the average
number of days with rainfall per year is 84, there are interannual variations.

The annual averages mentioned are obtained from data provided by INUMET for the period
1991-2020. The following table shows the monthly averages for each parameter.

3.1.2 Geology
According to the Geological Chart of Uruguay at scale 1:500,000 (Preciozzi et al., 1985), in the
area affected by the enterprise emerges a single geological unit, from the Pleistocene.

This unit corresponds to the Libertad Formation, characterized by the presence of mudolites,
loess and fangolites with variable percentage of sands and clays, brown to reddish-brown.

Field characterization

Within the framework of the feasibility studies of the project under study, in April 2018, the firm
INSUELOS carried out a geotechnical characterization of the affected area. The different strata
of the subsoil were identified, as well as the water table and eventual rock roof. The findings of
this study that are relevant to the effects of soil characterization are described below.

Eight perforations were made, the locations of which are presented in the following figure. They
were executed with a manual and mechanized rotating equipment, including SPT tests and
collection of altered samples at each meter of drilling progress.

Figure 3-5: Location of the drillings carried out (Source: INSUELOS, 2018).
From the total samples collected, a representative number was chosen for laboratory analysis.
The processed samples were selected in such a way that it was possible to characterize the

different strata found in the perforations. The mentioned strata were identified in the field
through the tactile-visual description of the materials.

The following tests were carried out on the selected samples: determination of natural humidity,
granulometric analysis and determination of Atterberg limits (liquid limit and plastic limit).
Based on the information obtained from the granulometric analysis and the determinations of
Atterberg limits, the classification of each processed sample was carried out using the Unified
Soil Classification System (SUCS).

The soil profile is characterized by an upper layer of black organic soil whose thickness varies
between 0.50 m (B03) and 1.50 m (B08), followed by an alternation of clay layers of varied
colorations (brown, gray, greenish gray), of variable consistencies in depth and from soft to very
hard depending on the location on the property. The clay strata present at all points variable
contents of calcium carbonate.

At all points, the rejection condition was reached in the SPT tests, at depths ranging from 9 to
13 m.

In drilling BO3, between 13 and 15 m deep, the presence of fully weathered rock was
determined, identifying rock clasts in a clay matrix.

With respect to the plasticity of the soils, 50 samples of the 85 analyzed are classified as clays of
high compressibility SUCS, presenting high to very high plasticities. These soils usually have high
expansive potential, characterized by the occurrence of strong contraction-retraction processes
in the face of important changes in their moisture content.

At the time of the fieldwork, the presence of water table levels was detected at points BO1, BO4
and BO8, located between elevations 22 and 23.

3.1.5 Air quality

This characterization of air quality is based on the analysis of data from monitoring stations,
which is presented in ANNEX I.

The air quality monitoring station closest to the site of implementation of the project
corresponds to the "Barradas" station, belonging to DINACEA, which is located near the
Interbalnearia Route and Barradas Street, about 7.7 km away from the proposed project. Since
it does not have complete data for all the parameters of interest, other stations that are
considered representative were also considered, namely: "Tres Cruces", "Ciudad Vieja" and
"Curva de Maroñas", belonging to the air quality monitoring network of the Municipality of
Montevideo.

For each parameter and station, the completeness of the data was analyzed in order to
determine its representativeness. From this analysis, only the Tres Cruces and Curva de Maroñas
stations have more than 75% of data recorded for NO2 for years. In the case of PM2.5, only the
Ciudad Vieja station has several years with more than 75% of data recorded. For PM10, the Tres
Cruces station presents years that meet the completeness criterion, and for SO2, the Barradas
station presents a year with more than 75% data. In addition, only in the case of PM2.5 is there
three years of complete data, as required. For NO2, PM10 and SO2, data from years that meet
the completeness criterion, even if they are less than three, are used. As a general rule, the best
data in terms of completeness are used for all approaches.

In general, for the calculation of hourly averages, the 0.90th percentile of the hourly values of
each year of complete data is considered. If there is more than one year with complete data,
the maximum is taken, conservatively. For the calculation of daily averages, the 0.90th
percentile of the daily averages for each complete year is considered and then, if more than one
year of complete data is available, the average of these values is taken. For the characterization
of the baseline of annual average concentration, the daily average of the hourly values is

3.2 BIOTIC MEDIUM
3.2.1 Terrestrial biota
Conservation priorities within Uruguay's eco-regions

The report "Conservation priorities within the eco-regions of Uruguay" (Brazeiro and Soutullo,
2012), identifies priority sites for conservation within each of Uruguay's eco-regions.

The criteria for prioritizing areas for biodiversity conservation were established taking into
account two important antecedents, in which national objectives linked to biodiversity
conservation are defined: the National Biodiversity Strategy, which responds to a commitment
assumed by the country before the Convention on Biological Diversity (ratified in Uruguay by
Law 16.408 of August 27, 1993) and Law 17.234 of August 22, 1993. February 2000, the creation
of the National System of Protected Areas (SNAP).

Based on this background, the following prioritization criteria were established: 1) species
richness; 2) ecosystem services; 3) threatened ecosystems. The criteria were evaluated in binary
form, assigning the value "1" if it is satisfactorily met (according to the criterion detailed in each
case), or "0" if it is not satisfied. Based on these criteria, ecosystems of maximum and high
priority by eco-region were identified and mapped with high spatial resolution, covering a total
of 12.2% of the national territory.

In the Figure 3-9 the map of conservation priorities obtained for the Graven eco-region of Saint
Lucia is presented. 15.3% of the eco-region was identified as a top and high priority (2 and 3
criteria). The venture is located in a low priority area because it does not meet any of the
conditions established as a prioritization criterion.

important bird populations are concentrated, since in these sites they find rest and food (Figure

Environmental characterization of the property under study

The project will be implemented on a site ofthe Free Trade Zone of Parque de las Ciencias, which
will be expanded to include the project premises. This extension already has authorization from
DINACEA (Ministerial Resolution 269/2020, Ministry of Environment). Part of the activities of
the implementation of the Free Trade Zone include the movement of soils, leveling of the
property and construction of internal perimeter roads. These activities will remove the
vegetation present, altering the biotic environment. For the purposes of the Teros project, the
data center will begin its construction on a property with a practically non-existent biotic
environment.

Likewise, within the framework of the feasibility studies of this venture, on March 23, 2018, a
field campaign was carried out by technicians from TETRA Consultores, in order to obtain a direct
vision and with primary information about the ecological values of the analysis area.

The property of the proposed project, at the date of the campaign can only be recognized as an
abandoned cultivation property, finding vegetation typical of naturalized environments that do
not recover in the form of natural meadow, but present elements of flora typical of
environments degraded by anthropic activities. This type of naturalized environment is the one
that is mentioned that is found at several points in the project environment.

The largest area of the property is covered by pastures, followed by wet grasslands, with the
other components identified being less frequent and punctual in the area.

Potential, priority and threatened species

fia estudio ingeniería ambiental 29
w

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STUDY — TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

Based on information available from DINAMA and national listings (Soutullo et al. 2013), a
potential analysis of tetrapod fauna species (amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals) that due
to their known distribution could make use of the study area, given the knowledge of the
environment and the biology of the species, was carried out. This analysis was performed by
TETRA Consultores.

Taking into account the conditions of the property of interest, it is established that the priority
species that could be present in the property are amphibians and birds. Considering the urban
environment of the proposed project and the state of the property naturalized with degradation
characteristics, no priority reptiles or mammals that may be found in the area of interest are
identified.

Specifically for the Amphibian group, of the potential species, only two could be found within
the area of interest: the Common Frog and the Darwin's Toad, the latter being a nationally
threatened species.

As for birds, it is considered that seven of the priority species could be found using the property:
the Ceniciento Hawk, the Tachuri Canela, the Grey-crowned Capuchin, the Domino, the Carao,
the Grey-headed Monterita and the Volatinero. The first four are listed as Vulnerable globally
and only the Grey-crowned Capuchin at the national level.

3.3 ANTHROPIC ENVIRONMENT

This characterization of the anthropic environment is based on the social perception report
carried out by the consulting firm Rivero-Quirino, which is presented in ANNEX VII.

3.3.1 Definition of the area of influence

As defined by the International Finance Corporation (IFC), the area of influence is the area likely
to be affected by cumulative impacts and planned development of the project, and areas likely
to be affected by development impacts that, although unplanned, may be predictable by the
project and may occur thereafter. It not only includes the surface of land in which the project is
immersed, but also all social groups of individuals, communities, companies, organizations and
government entities that are related.

The area of influence of the Teros project is delimited by Camino de los Horneros to the West,
Carrasco stream to the East, Route 102 to the North and Avenue to the Rambla Costanera to the
South. This area is located in a suburban area of the department of Canelones, which presents
an important dynamism, with projection of several industrial and logistics ventures.

Within the defined area of influence is the municipality of Nicolich-Ciudad Liber Seregni and its
urban areas -microregion of influence- the neighborhoods Colonia Nicolich, Aeroparque, Santa
Teresita, and the private neighborhood Colinas de Carrasco.

3.3.2 Origin and development of the area of influence

The first settlers in the area appeared in 1925, when a private man bought a field of 550 hectares
(what is now the Colonia Nicolich neighborhood) and divided it into farms. Medianeros and
tenants, Orientals and European immigrants, were forming an agricultural area, which supplied
the growing city of Montevideo.

In the 40s the construction of the Carrasco International Airport begins and results in another
demographic change. The area begins to present a more urban profile, although with low
population density, mainly inhabited by workers from the Paso Carrasco and airport industries,

30

ia) estudio ingenieria ambiental
w

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STUDY — TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

with families that inhabit a territory without basic services. At the same time, a population
dedicated to intensive agriculture is also consolidated.

In the sixties other properties in the area were divided and Colonia Lamas was born, and the
Santa Teresita and Villa Aeroparque neighborhoods. Although this process of population growth
was not accompanied by a development of infrastructure and services.

During the 90s and the beginning of the XXI century, the area received a massive wave of new
settlers, expelled from the metropolitan area due to the different economic crises that the
country was going through, occupying abandoned private land. At the same time, a new
modality of urbanization made up of private neighborhoods was also taking hold.

For almost two decades the area has been going through processes of change, which have to do
with the coexistence and coexistence of multiple identities, different territories and social
groups, and with the potential that the area has to become a logistics and industrial territory.
The attraction of investments and the location of enterprises changed the landscape and
reconfigured the social and economic fabric. In the corridors of Routes 101 and 102 were located
large enterprises of commercial, industrial and logistics activities, which managed to constitute
the area as an industrial-logistics and service enclave. Likewise, this series of transformations
coexist with a scenario of poverty, exclusion, urban and housing precariousness, linked to the
conditions of infrastructure, equipment, accessibility and connectivity.

3.3.3 Institutional presence and daily life

With the creation of the Local Board of Colonia Nicolich, in 2006, local and departmental
governments began to have a greater approach to the territory, and in 2010 the Municipality
was created. These administrative changes made possible the beginning of urban planning,
through local government and citizen participation in local decisions, and reinforced
institutionality, citizen participation and government capacity.

The municipality of Colonia Nicolich articulates the different State agencies, with the aim of
unifying services and promoting the installation of services absent in the territory, in addition to
strengthening the social fabric of the locality.

This can be seen, for example, in the case of the Aeroparque neighborhood. In 2005 the
neighborhood had only one educational center of primary level and, with the development of
the presence of the State and the neighborhood organization, it was possible to buy a property
to build there the current educational pole of Aeroparque, in which it was sought to centralize
in one place all educational levels. It has an accompaniment house, which serves children from
45 days old to 2 years old, and has a preschool CAIF that serves those children from 2 years to 5
years old. A new primary school, a UTU CEA (Associated Educational Center) and, finally, a
secondary school were also built. This allows to generate synergies from an early age with the
students of the different cycles.

3.3.4 Economic activity and land use

The economic activities and the different uses that were given to the land in the study area
varied over the years.

The traditional land use of the area was small agriculture, typical of the suburban areas of the
metropolitan area. Since the end of the last century, land use has been transformed with the
installation of industries and logistics ventures that have been consolidated especially from the
operation of Zona América. The predominant land use today is logistics, services and also urban
consolidation.

The "Nicolich and Route 101 Plan", which corresponds to the Territorial Planning Instrument
that regulates the area, promotes economic development based on the implementation of new
productive activities and services in the area, since the area presents competitive advantages in
the metropolitan context and must be managed responsibly in harmony with the environment
and the landscape.

Departmental scale

The department of Canelones has 4,536 km2 of extension and is located in the south of the
country. It is bordered on the north by Florida, on the east by Lavalleja and Maldonado, on the
south by the Atlantic Ocean, on the southeast by Montevideo and on the west by San José. In
the western and northern part the limit with the other departments is formed by the Santa Lucia
River, and in the eastern part it is formed by the Cuchilla Grande and the Arroyo Solis Grande.

According to the data obtained in the population census carried out in 2011, the department of
Canelones has a total population of 520,187 inhabitants, being the second most populous
department in the country (The evolution of the number of inhabitants is shown in the Board
3-10). Of the total, 471,968 inhabitants are in urban areas and 48,219 in rural areas.

The department has a mostly female population, concentrated in the highest age levels (267,063
are women and 253,124 are men). The Figure 3-13 indicates the population structure of
Canelones, according to sex and age.

3.3.6 Traffic and roads

The area of influence is characterized by the axis of routes 101 and 102 -the most important
transit routes in the area-, which serve as corridors to the eastern exit of Montevideo and
connect with the Interbalnearia Route (Route 200), Route 8 and Route 5, generating a particular
area that goes between the Carrasco and Pando streams. and which includes several population
centers.

Route 101 is completely arranged in the department of Canelones, forming part of the accesses
to the city of Montevideo. It connects the Avenida de las Américas with the city of Pando, the
Interbalnearia Route and Routes 8 and 102. Route 102 links Routes 5, 6, 7, 8, 101, Interbalnearia
and Giannattasio Avenue. The Interbalnearia Route (RI) connects Carrasco Airport with the
resorts east of Montevideo. Route 8 connects the departments of Montevideo, Canelones,
Lavalleja, Treinta y Tres and Cerro Largo. Route 5 connects the departments of Montevideo,
Florida, Durazno, Tacuarembó and Rivera.

The viewshed associated with a point (which is defined by its latitude, longitude, and height z in
a given coordinate system) is the set of all points on the earth's surface from which there is a
direct line of sight to the reference point.

The determination of the gross visual basin associated with the project under study is done
considering the highest point of the same, and a terrain model in which at first the elements
outside the topography (buildings, trees, etc.) are not added. Based on this, it is calculated, by
simple geometry and taking into account the curvature of the earth and the atmospheric
attenuation to visibility, the points of the terrain from which the undertaking could be visualized.

3.4.2 Archaeological heritage

Within the framework of the feasibility studies of this undertaking, in April 2018 Florines &
Toscano carried out an archaeological study with recognition of the site. The main findings are
listed below. Although this study was carried out with a focus on the current register 47,763,
due to the results obtained and the characteristics of the property, it is considered that the
surveys carried out and the information presented below are also valid for registers Nos. 47,827
to 47,832 of the 7th Cadastral Section of the department of Canelones, which are currently part
of the property of the proposed enterprise.

The affected site, like its surroundings, is very impacted by different anthropization processes,
in particular the development of tillage of the land. The study area is covered by vegetal tapestry,
with the development of high grasslands and several sectors covered by foxtail (Cortaderia
selloana). The aforementioned plant expression prevented the archaeological observation of
the terrain. However, it was possible to control small accessible sectors due to the existence of
small pedestrian paths, and furrows of old crops.

In the small sectors of archaeological visibility, small erosions, furrows and paths, no
archaeological or prehistoric or historical evidence was detected.

Considering the lack of particular antecedents of the area and considering that it is a very
anthropized property, if there had been archaeological vestiges, they would have already been
removed from the site. Consequently, it is concluded that it is very likely that no archaeological
materials will be detected.

3.4.3 Social perception

Within the framework of the study of this venture, the consultant Rivero Quirino carried out a
study of social perception, whose general objective was to inquire about the perception
(knowledge, expectation and visions) that the community of the area of influence has about the
proposed project.

It should be noted that the social perception study was carried out in 2021 for the original
project, presenting the venture as the construction of two data center buildings. In this original
scenario, the consumption of drinking water would be higher and industrial effluents would be
generated continuously, in addition to the fact that there would be more sources of noise
emission and emissions into the atmosphere in case of emergency. It is understood that the
changes made to the project are favorable for the social perception regarding the aspects of
effluent management, emissions to the atmosphere and traffic affectation.

The results of the study are taken as valid for the current project.
Methodology

The methodology followed for the evaluation of this impact is presented in detail in the full
report of the social perception study presented in ANNEX VII.

In summary, for the Social Perception and Impact Study, a methodology was carried out that
combined different qualitative and quantitative techniques of data collection and analysis,
which allowed a comprehensive and exhaustive study of the perception of the actors related to
the project.

Data collection was carried out through semi-structured in-depth interviews, based on thematic
guides that allowed obtaining comparable and analyzable information on the perceptions,
expectations and fears of the various audiences of interest. The sample of the interviews was
initially made up of qualified informants, recognized as local references, with a comprehensive

42

ia) estudio ingenieria ambiental
w

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STUDY — TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

vision of the needs and expectations of the environment, which allowed an approach to other
actors in the community, through the snowball technique.

Results

The level of knowledge that the interviewees presented about the project varies depending on
the group involved. The neighbors, merchants and local referents claim to know the aspects
related to the Teros project, especially those related to the purchase of land in the Science Park
for its installation, while the political authorities express a greater degree of knowledge.

The vast majority of the interviewees viewed the installation of the Teros project positively.
Among the aspects that stand out the most are: the expectation for the generation of local labor
and greater commercial activity, the recognition that the town can have and the improvement
in infrastructure for the area.

Regarding the hope of hiring local labor, the interviewees emphasized that expectations are
slight, given the experience with industrial enterprises that have been installed in recent years
(companies often already come with their employees or demand skilled labor, which is not in
the area). Despite these experiences, the interviewees point out that the generation of jobs,
both direct and indirect, as well as the commercial movement that could occur with the
installation of this project, are the most positive aspects of entrepreneurship.

Likewise, the interviewees specify the difficulties that are generated with the traffic driven by
the industries installed there, given that the area has been going through changes in its
productive development for several years, which have been modifying the landscape and
mobility, and emphasize that this is the main concern when an enterprise of these
characteristics plans to settle in the territory.

In addition, in the community there is a particular interest in the care of the environment, so the
management of effluents and emissions into the atmosphere are part of the concerns expressed
by the interviewees, who also point out that they have not had problems with the companies
installed, and that even environmental care and the improvement of public spaces are sought
together with them.

The arrival of the Teros project was seen in the interviews as a boost to the projection of the city
at the departmental and national level. All the interviewees, but especially those linked to
commerce, education and local government, emphasized that the venture can give new
recognition to the town, which will host the project in Uruguay and will be able to position itself
in that way.

In conclusion, the interviewees express a favorable position to the construction of the Teros
project, which would give, as they specified, a "positioning of the locality", and would improve
the infrastructure of the internal streets of the city. Many of the interviewees, both politically
and socially, recognize in the community-municipality-companies relationship a key element for
the development of the area.

4.1 INTRODUCTION

The information presented in this document is for the purpose of conducting a complete
evaluation of the potential benefits and impacts of the proposed data center project. As
indicated throughout the document, many of these scenarios are very highly unlikely to occur,
but are nonetheless included in this analysis as a matter of due diligence. Whenever necessary,
mitigation plans are developed to address impacts, no matter how unlikely, in a safe and
responsible manner, and in compliance with applicable local laws and regulations.

Impacts of medium significance, especially those that require quantification for the
determination of their eligibility and the effectiveness of their management measures, as well
as impacts of high significance, are evaluated in section 5.

4.6 IMPACTS OF LOW SIGNIFICANCE
4.6.1 Impacts of the construction phase

Id 1 and Id 2 - Displacement of species by physical presence of the work, and loss of ecosystems
by physical presence of the work

The proposed site for the implementation of the enterprise is a naturalized patch post-
agricultural disturbance, with low ecological value, like so many others that are in its
environment. Two species of amphibians and seven species of birds were identified as priority
species potentially present. These species, if presented on the property, may migrate to other
naturalized patches of the environment when the works begin, so no management or mitigation
measures are required. It is understood in this way that the impacts are of low significance, being
admissible in the receiving environment.

Likewise, the activities of soil movement, leveling of the property and construction of perimeter
roads, which will remove the vegetation present, altering the biotic environment, will be carried
out by PDLC within the framework of the expansion of the Free Trade Zone, which already has
authorization from DINACEA. For the purposes of the Teros project, the data center will begin
its construction on a site with a practically non-existent biotic environment.

Id 3 - Modification of land uses due to the physical presence of the work

The physical presence and execution of the work will imply a modification of the use of the land.
At present, the property where the enterprise will be implemented is in disuse, but its last
productive use was agricultural cultivation. The magnitude of the impact is considered high, but
the importance of the factor is low, so the significance of the impact is considered low as well.

56

Gia) estudio ingeniería ambiental
w

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STUDY — TEROS PROJECT ELEANOR APPLICATIONS S.R.L.

It is then understood that the impact is admissible in the receiving environment without the
need to implement additional management measures.

Id 4 - Alteration of the landscape by physical presence of the work

The physical presence of the work will generate an alteration to the landscape that can be
perceived from the immediate proximity to the site of emplacement. It is understood that the
magnitude of this distortion will be medium, given that the property is within PDLC, where a
considerable number of buildings have been built whose construction process has requirements
similar to that of the enterprise under study. It is understood that the impact is admissible in the
receiving environment without the need to implement additional management measures.

Id 5 - Loss of archaeological heritage due to physical presence of the work

The physical presence and execution of the work could trigger the loss of archaeological heritage
present at the site. The characterization of the site by experts in the field through cabinet work
and field visit, does not warn of the presence of archaeological heritage, understanding that the
probability of it being is very low given the use of the land in the property. In this way, it is
understood that the magnitude of the impact will be low, being also low the significance, not
requiring management or mitigation measures to make the impact admissible in the receiving
environment.

Id 6 - Negative social perception by physical presence of the work

Although the social perception of the presence of the work is a potential impact of relevance, it
is understood that the impact will be of low significance, because it is of medium magnitude and
is temporary.

Likewise, the potential social impacts identified on the community of Nicolich-Ciudad Liber
Seregni are presented below, which arise from a combination of the analysis of the results of
the interviews and the analysis of the main characteristics of the project.

together with the impacted factor, its description, and its significance. The evaluation criterion

On the other hand, positive social impacts were also identified for the construction phase, such
as the generation of jobs, with medium significance, and commercial dynamism, with low
significance. The community welcomes the generation of jobs, but its expectation is affected by
previous experiences in which few jobs were generated for the local population. In turn, the
development of the construction phase can generate an increase in commercial and economic
activity in the area. The community has a certain expectation that the movement generated by
the construction of the project can boost the economy.

According to the study of social perception carried out on the area of influence, as indicated in
section 3.4.3, the community values in a good way the industrial development that the area has
had in recent years. In addition, the company has a dedicated team that works closely with the
local community to create an engagement strategy that is tailored to help with identified
community needs.

For all this, it is understood that the impact will be admissible in the receiving environment
without the need to implement additional management measures.

Id 7, Id 8 and Id, 9 - Deterioration of the landscape due to inadequate management of waste
assimilable to domestic, deterioration of the landscape due to inadequate management of
ROC, and contamination of surface water due to inadequate management of hazardous waste

Solid waste assimilable to domestic, construction construction waste (ROC), and hazardous
waste will be generated. The waste will be segregated and stored according to the type. Those
assimilable to domestic and construction waste will be transferred to the final disposal sites
agreed with the Intendancy. For hazardous waste, companies authorized by the Ministry of
Environment will be hired for its transport and disposal.

Considering these management measures, it is understood that the impacts will be of low
significance, not requiring additional measures to be admissible in the receiving environment.

Id 10 - Surface water pollution from improper management of wastewater from concrete
washing

Wastewater will be generated from the washing of the half cane of the mixer trucks (premixed
concretes). Improper management of these effluents could affect the quality of surface water
in water courses near where the property drains. In order to avoid this, the project will have a
treatment pool with a capacity of 29 m3, divided into 3 interconnected compartments. The first
will be intended for sedimentation, so as to reach the required concentration of suspended
solids (maximum 700 mg / L). The other two pools will be intended for pH control and
adjustment in order to obtain a maximum pH of 9. The effluent will be disposed of in compliance
with the standards of Decree 253/979 and amendments. The sludge will be treated as solid
waste.

It is then understood that the impact is of low significance, given that the venture has adequate
management measures incorporated in the project phase, which will achieve the admissibility
of the residual impact, without the need for additional measures.

Id 11 - Surface water pollution from improper management of commissioning wastewater

Industrial wastewater will be generated during the commissioning of the 4 stages of the
construction phase, coming from the cleaning and passivation process of the piping systems of
the closed cycle cooling water. The inadequate management of these effluents could affect the
quality of surface water in water courses near where the property drains, since it will have
cleaning products and dirt from the pipe system. For this reason, at the time of generation there
will be a system for collecting this wastewater, which will be accumulated in transportable tanks
or tanker trucks and then sent to an authorized management company for final disposal.

It is understood that the impact is of low significance, given that the venture has adequate
management measures incorporated in the project phase, which will achieve the admissibility
of the residual impact, without the need for additional measures.

Id 12 - Surface water pollution due to inadequate management of domestic wastewater

Wastewater assimilable to domestic water will be generated, corresponding to those generated
in the hygienic services of the workshop. The hygienic services in principle will divert their
effluents to an impermeable tank, which will be emptied regularly by a duly authorized
barometric company. Chemical toilets will be available on work fronts away from the facilities.
These will comply with a permanent maintenance system, whose emptying will be carried out
through companies that have the relevant authorizations. Once the domestic wastewater
discharge line of the PDLC WWTP is installed, wastewater assimilable to domestic corresponding

Id 13 - Surface water pollution from improper rainwater management

The uncovering of the land and movement of soils may generate dragging of suspended material
due to rain events, which could result in an increase in the concentration of suspended material
in the water courses near where the property drains. Among the works associated with the
expansion of PDLC are the rainwater buffer lagoons and temporary diversion ditches in the
perimeter of the planned blockage, which will lead the rainwater to these lagoons. This
infrastructure is the one that will be used during the construction phase for the rainwater
management of the proposed project. The reduction of the speed of the water in the lagoons
will tend to facilitate the sedimentation of the suspended material, reducing its concentration.

It is understood that the magnitude of this impact will be medium, and that it may occur only in
intense rainfall events, thus having a low probability of occurrence and a short duration. In this
way, the significance of the impact is low, being admissible in the receiving environment without
the need to incorporate additional management measures.

Id 14 and Id 15 - Impact on local traffic by traffic induced on site, and deterioration of road
infrastructure by traffic induced on site

A study of the impact on local traffic due to induced traffic was carried out both for the
construction phase and for the operation phase, in charge of Engineer Vial Diego Gagliardi. It is
presented in ANNEX VI. On the other hand, there is a specific traffic study for the roundabout
access to the Science Park, by the consultancy CSI Ingenieros, carried out for the original project
(see section 1.7 of the DP). It is presented in ANNEX IX. The conclusions of this report are taken,
but it is taken into account that the project proposed at present presents significantly less
induced traffic than the original project. Below are the main findings of both studies for the
construction phase.

The proposed project will generate vehicle traffic in the area, either during construction or
operation, which will not significantly affect the current situation of the National Routes. After
analyzing the service levels of the routes involved, with and without a project, it was concluded
that, if PDLC is used as an access point, it is understood that the project does not require
mitigation measures on local routes and streets. On the other hand, if it is accessed by G.
Rodriguez, the corresponding permit must be processed before the Ministry of Transport and
Public Works in case it is necessary to use unauthorized vehicles on Route IB. In addition,
consideration should be given to agreeing to an improvement with the MTOP at the intersection
of Route IB with G. Rodriguez, given that its current performance is not adequate for the safety
expected by the proposed project. In addition, speed control measures must be agreed with the
Intendancy to ensure that trucks do not exceed the established limits; The time of entry and exit
should be strict and try to minimize simultaneous circulation in opposite directions.

Regarding the analysis of service level and delays of the PDLC access roundabout, the trips of
heavy vehicles generated by the project during the construction phase do not greatly affect the
average delays and service levels existing in the roundabout, with the most affected approach
providing access to the PDLC. The departure of the personnel associated with the work is the
one that generates the greatest impact on the current circulating traffic, modifying the overall

Id 35 and Id 36 - Surface water pollution from oil spills, and surface water pollution from
chemical input spills

During the operation there could be small accidental spills of hydrocarbons associated with the
maintenance of generators, or chemical inputs. Preventive measures will be applied to minimize
the likelihood of spills occurring.

The areas destined for the loading of fuel for mechanical and electrical yards will have a spill
containment system. Each system will have a parapet and a spill containment chamber with leak
detection sensors. If a fuel spill occurs, the liquids will be pumped into a tanker truck.

For fuel distribution systems, a containment system consisting of double-walled pipes is
contemplated that will lead the potential spill gravitationally to the spill containment chamber
of the cargo area. In case this is not possible, the removal of the confined fuel through pumping
to a tanker truck is contemplated, to later transfer it as hazardous waste until its final disposal
by a duly authorized company.

The generators will have attached fuel tanks, which have a double-walled containment system
that is capable of containing a volume of spills greater than 100% of the capacity of the pond.

When a spill occurs, the liquids will be conducted to the containment chamber of the fuel loading

5. ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT

This section evaluates environmental impacts of medium significance that require it, as well as

5.1 IMPACTS OF MEDIUM SIGNIFICANCE
5.1.1 Impacts of the construction phase
Id 18 - Impact on the health and well-being of the nearby population by noise emissions

During the construction phase of the project, the machinery to be used on the site will generate
noise emissions that may increase the sound pressure level in the vicinity of the project,
potentially generating discomfort in the nearby population. It is understood that the impact is
of high magnitude, having a medium significance. Beyond the fact that the impact can be
adequately managed and mitigated, an evaluation of this is carried out based on predictive
models, in order to have a better approximation to the potential impact on the sound pressure
level in the vicinity of the project. For this, the methodology presented in the ISO 9613-2
standard for outdoor noise propagation is used.

According to the characterization carried out in the DP and the information obtained for
machinery associated with the movement of soils to be executed by PDLC, the emission levels
of the different equipment will be those presented in the table below.
